title: ABUELA CLAUS (Grandma Claus)
logline: On December 23rd, when the legendary mall Santa has a breakdown with 800 children in line, VALENTINA RIOS -- the HR director who spent a year documenting the case to cancel the program -- is forced into the suit and must save Christmas, and the very first child to sit on her lap is the nine-year-old granddaughter she has never met, who recognizes her from LinkedIn and will not leave the chair until Valentina fixes what she broke.
cleanScript:
Pitch: ABUELA CLAUS (Grandma Claus).
Format: Feature Film.

On December 23rd, when the legendary mall Santa has a breakdown with 800 children in line, VALENTINA RIOS -- the HR director who spent a year documenting the case to cancel the program -- is forced into the suit and must save Christmas, and the very first child to sit on her lap is the nine-year-old granddaughter she has never met, who recognizes her from LinkedIn and will not leave the chair until Valentina fixes what she broke.

VALENTINA RIOS, 52, is the HR director who spent a year building the case to eliminate the mall Santa program. Inefficient. Over-budget. A cost center with no measurable ROI. Her elimination report is 47 pages and airtight.

On December 23rd, the legendary AURELIO -- 27 years in the suit, beloved by every child who has ever sat on his lap -- has a public breakdown in front of 800 children and every camera in Mexico City. The specific cause: no one in the company knew his name. Valentina's report referred to him only as "Resource 7."

Valentina is the only executive close enough to the suit. She is forced into it.

The chair is barely warm when ISABELLA, age 9, sits down. She looks at Valentina for exactly three seconds. She says: I know who you are. She produces a printed LinkedIn screenshot from her coat pocket. She says: you're the woman who texted my mom "Christmas is tomorrow" eight years ago and never came back. She places it on the armrest. She says: I want to tell you what I want for Christmas and you are going to listen.

Isabella is Valentina's granddaughter. She has never met Valentina. She has a folder.

Valentina attempts every management technique she owns: reframing, timeline restructuring, stakeholder alignment, de-escalation. Isabella counters each one. When Valentina tries to end the shift early, Isabella shows her the second document: a screenshot of the elimination report. She says: if you leave before 6 PM, my mom's friend -- she gestures to a journalist twelve feet away who waves pleasantly -- gets this. The negotiation is conducted entirely in Valentina's Santa voice, in front of an increasingly charmed queue of parents who believe they are witnessing an improvised Christmas special.

Aurelio, still in civilian clothes and sobbing into a Pollo Feliz in the food court, cannot stop himself. He begins coaching Valentina through an earpiece: this child has a dog named Manchas, that one's parents are divorcing. This girl wants the same thing she wanted last year. Valentina is simultaneously arguing with a crying man through an earpiece and performing jolly for the children.

With 90 minutes left, Valentina stops. She asks Aurelio -- through the earpiece, in a whisper, while a child waits on her lap -- why he knows all of their names. Aurelio says: because I came back the next year and asked. The child on her lap watches her face change. The child says: you're not Santa. Valentina says: I know. The child says: are you trying? Valentina says: yes. The child says: okay.

The journalist is doing a live segment. She has the elimination report. Valentina -- in the Santa suit, with 400 children still in line and Isabella sitting beside her on the armrest like a co-anchor -- is offered a choice in real time: defend the report on camera, or tell the truth about what December 23rd eight years ago actually cost her. What she does instead surprises everyone, including Isabella.

She chose work over her daughter's Christmas so many times that love stopped being a choice -- and she only realized it when a nine-year-old with a printout made it a condition of employment.

Comparable titles: No Se Aceptan Devoluciones , Un Padre No Tan Padre , Bad Santa

December 23rd theatrical release window is open in Mexican cinema -- no Mexican Christmas theatrical comedy with this specific engine is in announced development or recent release history. The estrangement-and-accountability story hits the cultural moment around family, absence, and working-parent guilt that resonates strongly for 2026 holiday audiences.

title: BAJO CONTROL (Under Control)
logline: When Mexico City's most meticulous parking attendant -- 15 years, zero incidents -- is assigned to the 5th basement level of Polanco's newest mega-mall, where no car stays where he parks it, his obsessive application of professional rigor to an unsolvable problem begins dismantling the careful fiction of certainty he has built his entire life around.
cleanScript:
Pitch: BAJO CONTROL (Under Control).
Format: Feature Film.

When Mexico City's most meticulous parking attendant -- 15 years, zero incidents -- is assigned to the 5th basement level of Polanco's newest mega-mall, where no car stays where he parks it, his obsessive application of professional rigor to an unsolvable problem begins dismantling the careful fiction of certainty he has built his entire life around.

DON AURELIO CASTILLO, 63, has 15 years and zero incidents at his back. His entire worth lives in his perfect record. When the mall's Gerente ARTURO CORTÉS assigns him to B5 -- knowing what B5 does -- he needs someone meticulous enough to keep clients from complaining, principled enough to never admit the truth.

B5 is the one place on earth where cars cannot be kept. Don Aurelio parks 22 cars with military precision, chalk-marking each space. Returns three hours later: all 22 cars rearranged. Not stolen -- present, relocated. He has the only card reader on B5. He reparks every car. Returns one hour later: every car back to its original position. He fills six pages of notes and decides the answer is more data.

He requisitions a structural engineer, a GPS specialist, the head of mall security. The engineer's laser returns a different reading every time. The GPS tracker shows one Nissan Versa simultaneously on B5 and in Guadalajara, both signals equally strong. The security chief watches 45 minutes of empty-level footage -- then looks up to find 22 cars parked there.

For the VIP opening gala, Don Aurelio manages 200 Polanco elite cars with perfect efficiency. Four hours later he retrieves the first car for a departing guest. Parked directly next to it: the 1998 Volkswagen Jetta he parked at his first job in Iztapalapa in 2011. He walks the level. Every car he has ever parked in 15 years is there. In perfect order. 847 cars. He does not panic. He opens his notebook to a fresh page and begins the log.

The comedy runs on a single devastating irony: Don Aurelio has never admitted he does not know something. His entire life is a performance of certainty. B5 is the first thing in 15 years that proves that performance was always fiction.

Comparable titles: Bienvenue chez les Ch'tis , Nosotros los Nobles , El Método Gronholm

Surreal comedy is an underserved lane in Mexican theatrical. The Polanco class commentary and the invisible working-class protagonist who maintains dignity while managing inexplicable chaos are immediately culturally resonant.

title: CASADOS POR ACCIDENTE (Accidentally Married)
logline: Two strangers wake up legally married in Cancun after a work conference party and have 48 hours to get an annulment before their real partners arrive to surprise them at the same resort.
cleanScript:
Pitch: CASADOS POR ACCIDENTE (Accidentally Married).
Format: Feature Film.

Two strangers wake up legally married in Cancun after a work conference party and have 48 hours to get an annulment before their real partners arrive to surprise them at the same resort.

ANA SALAS, 33, is an obsessively controlled HR director who has not made an impulsive decision since 2018. CARLOS VEGA, 35, is a spectacularly disorganized freelance photographer who makes every decision by feeling. They wake up legally married after a conference afterparty neither of them can fully reconstruct. Both have partners flying into Cancun in 48 hours to surprise them with romantic getaways. The annulment requires a judge who is only available in 48 hours. Every attempt to quietly fix the situation requires them to convincingly perform a happy marriage to everyone around them -- including the partners who have just arrived. The fake marriage turns out more real than anything either of them had before.

Comparable titles: Anyone But You , The Proposal , Bridesmaids

Anyone But You ($220M WW on $25M budget, 2023) proved accidental-romance comedy has massive current commercial appetite. The destination resort setting is proven theatrical territory. No current Spanish-language film occupies this exact lane.

title: DISCULPA DE OFICIO (Apology by Trade)
logline: When Mexico City's most methodical professional apologizer accepts a commission to deliver five apologies across the city before the main one, he spends the day accumulating, one appointment at a time, the complete story of his own abandonment -- and arrives at the last door knowing everything except that he is the person the story is about.
cleanScript:
Pitch: DISCULPA DE OFICIO (Apology by Trade).
Format: Feature Film.

When Mexico City's most methodical professional apologizer accepts a commission to deliver five apologies across the city before the main one, he spends the day accumulating, one appointment at a time, the complete story of his own abandonment -- and arrives at the last door knowing everything except that he is the person the story is about.

CARLOS RUEDA, 40s, has built an eleven-year career as Mexico City's most methodical professional apologizer. He is a Type 6 Loyalist who grew up fatherless and replaced that absence with protocol and structure. He trusts the file above everything, including himself.

A dying man named HECTOR RUEDA commissions Carlos to deliver five preliminary apologies across Mexico City before the main delivery. Hector designed the route in a specific order so that Carlos would understand the whole story before reaching the last door. Carlos reorders the appointments by geography for efficiency. This is his fatal professional error.

The film is an information comedy where the audience is ahead of the protagonist from the second stop. Carlos visits Hector's old business partner: 'He left the business to go find a woman.' Carlos writes: Client abandoned business for personal reasons. He visits Hector's sister -- who raised Carlos. He writes: Client concealed significant personal matter from family. He visits the church where a wedding was planned the week after Carlos was born. He visits the doctor who delivered Carlos and called Hector. He does not read the four pages together. He goes to the last address.

DOLORES opens the door. Carlos reads the final apology: 'For not finding you sooner.' She looks at him for a long moment. 'Did he tell you to come in order?' Carlos: 'I reordered by geography.' She says: 'Then you already know.' She lays a map on the table and traces each address with her finger. Carlos opens his notebook to page one. He reads his own notes from the morning straight through. He says: 'I will need to refile the engagement as a personal matter.' Dolores: 'Your father is still alive.' Carlos: 'I know.' He looks at his appointment book. 'He is the two o'clock.'

Comparable titles: Knives Out , No Se Aceptan Devoluciones , La Convencion

Information comedy with the audience ahead of the protagonist is having a global moment post-Knives Out. DISCULPA DE OFICIO delivers this structure with deep Mexican cultural specificity: the professional-armor wound of Mexican men and the absent-father story land with immediate cultural recognition in 2026, and the Mexico City scavenger-hunt architecture makes the film visually distinctive.

title: EL ALGORITMO NO MIENTE (The Algorithm Doesn't Lie)
logline: A veteran matchmaker launches an AI compatibility algorithm at an 800-guest gala and discovers its first result -- a 98.7% match between her ex-husband and her business partner -- live in front of every client she has.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL ALGORITMO NO MIENTE (The Algorithm Doesn't Lie).
Format: Feature Film.

A veteran matchmaker launches an AI compatibility algorithm at an 800-guest gala and discovers its first result -- a 98.7% match between her ex-husband and her business partner -- live in front of every client she has.

Carmen Vargas has spent 15 years as Mexico City's most trusted matchmaker, now launching her proprietary compatibility algorithm in a high-stakes streaming deal that will redefine the industry. At the launch gala -- 800 guests, cameras, the streaming executives in the front row -- the algorithm's very first result pairs her ex-husband Mario at 98.7% compatibility with her business partner Sofía. Mario, who has been quietly trying to reconnect with Carmen for months, is now officially matched to her closest colleague by Carmen's own creation. The irony is structural: she built a system that cannot see her own romantic blind spot, then ran it in public. Carmen has one gala to get through, a streaming deal to close, and two people she trusts most now being directed toward each other by her algorithm. The film's comedy engine is the collision between professional certainty and personal chaos: every tool she has for managing other people's romantic lives is useless on her own. The middle tracks Carmen trying to manage the social fallout while the algorithm keeps running, correctly. The climax forces her to either honor the algorithm's result (validating her professional work at personal cost) or discredit it (protecting herself by destroying the tool she built). The new normal: whether she keeps the algorithm or not, she has finally seen herself clearly for the first time.

Comparable titles: Hitch , Nosotros Los Nobles , Crazy Rich Asians

Algorithm fatigue and the offline dating renaissance are live cultural conversations in 2026. The AI-as-romantic-engine concept has zero Spanish-language entries -- this is first-mover position in a genre Netflix LatAm has identified as a 2026 priority.

title: EL COBRO (The Collection)
logline: A government mine inspector sent to recertify the site where she falsified a safety report 15 years ago discovers the Wixaritari guardian entity has been waiting -- and will collect the debt on her bloodline unless she destroys the career and freedom she built on the lie.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL COBRO (The Collection).
Format: Feature Film.

A government mine inspector sent to recertify the site where she falsified a safety report 15 years ago discovers the Wixaritari guardian entity has been waiting -- and will collect the debt on her bloodline unless she destroys the career and freedom she built on the lie.

Maria Elena Garza has spent 15 years building a career on a lie. When she was a junior mine inspector at the Santa Elena silver mine in Zacatecas, she filed a falsified incident report that buried evidence of a gas leak -- and buried the four miners who died in it. She removed the Wixaritari sacred boundary markers from her documentation, signed off on a clean certification, and never went back. The mine prospered. Her career advanced. Her daughter was born.

Fifteen years later, the mine must be recertified. The company sends Maria Elena because Roberto Saldivar, the site manager, has kept her falsified report all this time. She signs the new certification or he releases the original. It is not a choice -- it is a trap with a deadline.

The first night back at the mine, Maria Elena's daughter begins sleepwalking toward the shaft.

The Wixaritari guardian -- whose nierika (sacred portal-eye) the mine drilling violated -- has been waiting. It does not want confession. It does not want the certification stopped. It wants what the four miners' deaths purchased: a debt paid in bloodline. Every falsified report Maria Elena has ever signed is a receipt for a spiritual obligation that has been accruing for 15 years.

Saldivar holds the professional weapon. The entity holds her daughter. The certification is the only thing both of them need from her -- and the only way out of the trap is an act of public witness that will cost her career, her freedom, and her identity as the inspector who always signed clean.

A horror film about what it means to have built your life on a signature that was always a pact.

Comparable titles: The Wailing , Sinister , The Descent

The rules-based ritual horror lane is commercially proven: Talk to Me ($91.9M on a $4.5M budget) and Exhuma ($97M) both connected global audiences to indigenous spiritual mechanisms through a clear, specific logic. The Wixaritari sacred territory angle is culturally specific to Mexico and entirely untouched at theatrical or streaming scale. Mining horror is an open lane -- no Spanish-language property occupies the intersection of government institutional corruption and indigenous supernatural debt.

title: EL COPAL (The Copal)
logline: When a teenage boy burns his family's forbidden copal resin, his Oaxacan mother has 72 hours to contain the entity her ancestors imprisoned in the smoke before it possesses him permanently -- and the community elder who holds the containment knowledge has her own reasons for letting it complete.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL COPAL (The Copal).
Format: Feature Film.

When a teenage boy burns his family's forbidden copal resin, his Oaxacan mother has 72 hours to contain the entity her ancestors imprisoned in the smoke before it possesses him permanently -- and the community elder who holds the containment knowledge has her own reasons for letting it complete.

Beatriz Sandoval is a copal harvester from the Sierra Norte mountains of Oaxaca, keeper of a four-generation grove her family has tended and guarded since her great-grandmother's time. The grove contains something. The family has always known. The harvesting protocol is not superstition -- it is containment.

When Beatriz's teenage son Mateo burns a forbidden bundle of the sacred resin in a teenage act of rebellion he does not understand, the entity locked inside the smoke is released. Mateo begins to change. The possession moves slowly and precisely, targeting his sense of self before his body.

Beatriz needs the containment knowledge her community's eldest keeper holds -- but Dona Flora, the 80-year-old community elder, has her own undisclosed reasons for allowing the possession to complete. Flora is not a villain by malice; she is a woman who made a calculation decades ago about what the entity's release is worth to the community's survival, and she will not allow Beatriz to undo it.

The film is a race against absorption -- Beatriz trying to save her son while the community that has been her world closes against her. The horror is not just what is inside the smoke but what the people she trusted chose to keep from her.

Comparable titles: Vuelven / Tigers Are Not Afraid , Hereditary , Talk to Me

Pre-Hispanic folk horror is the lane Talk to Me and Vuelven opened. Oaxacan copal resin is culturally specific and unfilmed -- no competing project uses this material as its central horror engine. Netflix Mexico's commitment to authentic regional horror creates the market.

title: EL FORJADO (The Forged One)
logline: A struggling third-generation luchador who puts on his grandfather's legacy mask inherits a soul-debt created by a cheated apuesta forty years ago -- and the supernatural debt collector who has maintained his existence to collect it challenges him to settle it the only way the debt allows: inside the ring.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL FORJADO (The Forged One).
Format: Feature Film.

A struggling third-generation luchador who puts on his grandfather's legacy mask inherits a soul-debt created by a cheated apuesta forty years ago -- and the supernatural debt collector who has maintained his existence to collect it challenges him to settle it the only way the debt allows: inside the ring.

Hector Vargas is a struggling third-generation luchador who cannot fill his grandfather's boots. El Aguila Dorada was legendary -- unmasked once and came back. What the family has never spoken aloud is how. Forty years ago, El Aguila Dorada cheated an apuesta against a fighter named El Forjado -- forged from something not quite human, sustained by soul covenants -- and the match was rigged so El Aguila could keep his mask. El Forjado has waited since then, maintaining his unnatural existence through a series of smaller soul-contracts, keeping himself in the world long enough to collect what is owed from the last Vargas heir.

When Hector puts on his grandfather's legacy mask in desperation before a career-defining match he cannot afford to lose, the mask seals the debt to him. El Forjado surfaces. He does not come as a monster -- he comes as a challenger. The rules of lucha libre apply: the debt must be settled inside the ring.

Hector must fight a supernatural entity using his grandfather's stolen techniques against a collector who cannot be beaten by anything mortal and refuses to use any of the soul-covenant power during the match because honor is what makes the debt real. The horror is that El Forjado is right. Hector's grandfather cheated. The debt is legitimate. The only path out is to fight with everything he has and win honestly -- or lose and settle the account his grandfather avoided.

Comparable titles: The Wrestler  -- honor and identity stakes, Insidious  -- supernatural inheritance debt, Nacho Libre  -- lucha libre cultural register, inverted for horror

Lucha libre as a horror vehicle is completely untapped. The apuesta -- a real tradition where masks and careers are staked in matches -- is the most potent supernatural contract metaphor in Mexican culture. No competing film exists. The global appetite for culturally specific horror (Hereditary, Talk to Me, Vuelven) creates the acquisition lane.

title: EL HÉROE EQUIVOCADO (The Wrong Hero)
logline: A CDMX weatherman who has been wrong 70% of the time discovers he has been accidentally saving lives with his bad forecasts -- and when he finally starts getting it right, disasters start happening.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL HÉROE EQUIVOCADO (The Wrong Hero).
Format: Feature Film.

A CDMX weatherman who has been wrong 70% of the time discovers he has been accidentally saving lives with his bad forecasts -- and when he finally starts getting it right, disasters start happening.

RODRIGO SOTO, 38, is the most beloved weatherman in Mexico City -- not because he is accurate, but because his wrong forecasts consistently drive audiences away from places that then flood, collapse, or catch fire. He is a statistical anomaly and a beloved disaster. His producer knows the truth; his ratings depend on the lie. When RODRIGO meets a meteorologist who can actually read the sky, he falls in love with her -- and his desire to impress her makes him start getting forecasts right. Simultaneously, the weird protective halo his wrongness created begins to lift: accidents start happening in all the places he correctly predicted clear weather. Rodrigo must choose between becoming good at his job and being the accidental guardian angel he accidentally became. Bruce Almighty meets Groundhog Day, with Mexico City weather as the comedic engine.

Comparable titles: Bruce Almighty , Liar Liar , Evan Almighty

Accidental hero comedy with a high-concept premise is the most commercially durable register in mainstream comedy. No Spanish-language film has occupied the Bruce Almighty / Liar Liar territory. 2026 Mexican theatrical appetite for high-concept feel-good comedy is confirmed by MI.

title: EL MEJOR AMIGO DE MI MEJOR AMIGO (My Best Friend's Best Friend)
logline: When his best friend's childhood cuate returns to Mexico City after eight years abroad and immediately starts winning at friendship just by being himself, a 35-year-old man whose entire identity is being 'the funny one' launches an escalating one-sided war -- and discovers that without the laughs, he does not know who he is.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL MEJOR AMIGO DE MI MEJOR AMIGO (My Best Friend's Best Friend).
Format: Feature Film.

When his best friend's childhood cuate returns to Mexico City after eight years abroad and immediately starts winning at friendship just by being himself, a 35-year-old man whose entire identity is being 'the funny one' launches an escalating one-sided war -- and discovers that without the laughs, he does not know who he is.

Rodrigo, 35, has spent a decade being his best friend Marco's right-hand man -- the one who makes every room laugh, the one who always shows up, the one Marco calls first. His job title is incidental. His real job is being the funny one, and he is very good at it.

When Marco announces that his childhood best friend Emilio is moving back to Mexico after eight years in Barcelona, Rodrigo does not hear "old friend returning." He hears "replacement arriving."

Emilio is warm, effortless, and oblivious. He wins points without trying -- the same stories, the same references, the same mezcal, delivered without calculation. Every easy victory for Emilio costs Rodrigo something. Rodrigo escalates: he engineers situations to showcase his superior knowledge of Marco, he manufactures inside jokes in real time, he books better restaurants and orders the right tequila. None of it works. The more he performs, the more obvious the performance becomes.

At the midpoint, Marco calls it out. In front of both men, he names what is happening. Rodrigo cannot deny it. Emilio, understanding the stakes for the first time, stops retreating. He accepts the war. Now both men compete openly, and Marco is no longer flattered -- he is a person being treated like a trophy, and he is exhausted by it.

The war collapses at the worst possible moment: in public, in front of people who matter, in a situation Rodrigo cannot perform his way out of. What comes out instead is the truth: he does not know who he is when he is not making someone laugh. That confession, delivered in the wrong place at the wrong time, is more powerful than anything he has performed. The three men find a new dynamic. Rodrigo still makes them laugh -- but now he is allowed to be seen.

Comparable titles: I Love You Man , Step Brothers , Mirreyes vs. Godinez

Mexican theatrical comedy has no male friendship war film in this register. The cuate-bond comedy lane is open and proven at box office (Mirreyes vs. Godinez, No se Aceptan Devoluciones). The Step Brothers escalation engine has never been executed with specifically Mexican male friendship rituals at its core.

title: EL MEJOR MAN (The Best Man)
logline: Mexico City's most agreeable people-pleaser accidentally books himself as best man at three simultaneous weddings at the same Cancun resort -- and cannot cancel any of them without destroying the friendship that matters most.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL MEJOR MAN (The Best Man).
Format: Feature Film.

Mexico City's most agreeable people-pleaser accidentally books himself as best man at three simultaneous weddings at the same Cancun resort -- and cannot cancel any of them without destroying the friendship that matters most.

JAVIER LUNA, 33, is constitutionally incapable of saying no. When his three best friends from different phases of his life all get engaged within the same month and all ask him to be best man, he agrees to all three. When all three weddings end up at the same Cancun resort on the same weekend, Javier must execute three separate best man duties simultaneously while keeping the three friend groups from discovering each other. The three grooms are connected. The bridesmaids at two of the weddings know each other. And his girlfriend -- who does not know about any of this -- has also arrived at the resort. Three weddings, one weekend, zero spine, and one real relationship Javier is about to lose by refusing to be honest about who he actually is.

Comparable titles: Wedding Crashers , The Hangover , Game Night

Wedding Crashers ($285M WW) proved the comedy potential of multi-wedding chaos. No Spanish-language version of this exact premise exists. Cancun as the theatrical location is commercially proven for Mexican comedy.

title: EL MISMO VESTIDO (The Same Dress)
logline: Two women arrive at the same funeral in identical black dresses and discover the dead man maintained two complete households for twelve years -- one of them has been prepared for this moment for sixty years.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL MISMO VESTIDO (The Same Dress).
Format: Feature Film.

Two women arrive at the same funeral in identical black dresses and discover the dead man maintained two complete households for twelve years -- one of them has been prepared for this moment for sixty years.

Victoria, 45, arrives at her husband Roberto's funeral in the same black dress as another woman. The other woman is Senora Aurora, 87. Aurora is the legal first wife. Roberto maintained two complete households for twelve years -- Victoria's, which Roberto started when he was 35, and Aurora's, which Roberto started when he was 23 and never legally ended.

Aurora has been prepared for this moment for sixty years. She has the marriage certificate, the notarized property records, the family photographs that predate Victoria's existence. She holds every card and is entirely at peace with herself. She came to the funeral to close the book, not to start a fight.

Victoria must now navigate the funeral -- Roberto's family, his business partners, his other children -- while processing that twelve years of her adult life were built on a foundation she did not consent to examine. Aurora is not her enemy. Aurora is the map.

The comedy escalates from funeral to reception to estate reading to confrontation with Roberto's professional network, which has known about both households for years and said nothing. The audience for the revelation grows until it includes everyone Roberto trusted.

Comparable titles: Death at a Funeral , Matando Cabos , Four Weddings and a Funeral

No Mexican film has used bigamy discovery at a funeral as its central engine. The funeral-comedy format collapses social convention simultaneously. The 87-year-old first wife who is entirely prepared is a figure Mexican audiences will immediately recognize and root for.

title: EL MOSTO (The Must)
logline: A Jalisco tequila maestro discovers his family's fermentation process has trapped the dead in the mosto for a century -- and he is becoming addicted to tasting their voices.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL MOSTO (The Must).
Format: Feature Film.

A Jalisco tequila maestro discovers his family's fermentation process has trapped the dead in the mosto for a century -- and he is becoming addicted to tasting their voices.

Don Luis, fourth-generation maestro tequilero on a Jalisco estate his great-grandmother established, is tasting his way through a proprietary fermentation run when he hears it for the first time: a voice in the mosto. Not an auditory hallucination. A specific dead person with specific things to say.

His family has known. The process has always trapped consciousness at the threshold of fermentation. His ancestors decided this was acceptable -- the dead were resting, the tequila was extraordinary, and no one was asking to leave. Don Luis begins to disagree when the voices become insistent. And when he realizes he cannot stop tasting.

The opponent arrives from outside: Rafael Cardenas, acquisition director for a multinational spirits company. He has tasted the product. He has heard the dead. He is not disturbed. He wants to industrialize the arrangement at scale -- ten thousand trapped consciousnesses per production run. He genuinely believes death is an overcrowded waiting room and the mosto is a better alternative. Don Luis must stop a man who is in love with the horror he is enabling, while the addiction deepens with every session.

Comparable titles: Sinister , The Wailing , Hereditary

Tequila's global moment -- $12B export market, prestige brand expansion, international visibility -- makes this the rare horror film that arrives with a built-in cultural context the global audience already cares about. Jalisco craft culture has never been the setting for a theatrical horror film.

title: EL RELEVO (The Replacement)
logline: A Tulum freediving instructor recovers her missing student from an unmapped cenote chamber -- except the GoPro footage shows him breathing underwater at 32 meters for 14 minutes, and he came back wrong.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL RELEVO (The Replacement).
Format: Feature Film.

A Tulum freediving instructor recovers her missing student from an unmapped cenote chamber -- except the GoPro footage shows him breathing underwater at 32 meters for 14 minutes, and he came back wrong.

Claudia Torres rebuilt her dive school after losing her student Diego four years ago. She saw his behavioral warning signs before his fatal dive and was overruled by every instrument and professional. She rebuilt on one principle: she will never act on behavioral instinct alone.

Now she has a new student, Mateo, showing the same signs. When she trusts an instrument over her instinct, she sends him into the unmapped southern chamber of a sacred Maya cenote. Mateo comes back. The GoPro footage shows him breathing underwater for fourteen minutes at 32 meters.

Something in the cenote replaced Diego four years ago and has been replacing divers ever since. Hector Mora, the dive school owner, has maintained a cover-up of twelve prior replacements for eight years and needs Claudia gone before she exposes the cenote. The replacement of Mateo is incomplete -- she might still be able to reach him. But the cenote will finish it if she goes back in.

The horror engine is Claudia's wound turned rule: she suppressed her instinct because the instruments said she was wrong, and Diego died. Now the same suppressed instinct is the only thing that can save Mateo. The cenote does not want her to remember that.

Comparable titles: The Descent , Annihilation , Underwater

Tulum cenote tourism exploitation of pre-Classic Maya sacred geography is a live cultural conflict in 2026. Body horror about identity replacement taps AI-era anxieties about what makes a person real. The female-protagonist underwater-horror lane is proven and entirely empty in Spanish-language film.

title: EL REMEDIO (The Remedy)
logline: A faith healer's assistant discovers that her 12 years of miraculous cures have worked by unconsciously transferring illness to whoever she deems most dispensable -- just as the surrogate mother who raised her asks her to fire the mechanism consciously on a specific target.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL REMEDIO (The Remedy).
Format: Feature Film.

A faith healer's assistant discovers that her 12 years of miraculous cures have worked by unconsciously transferring illness to whoever she deems most dispensable -- just as the surrogate mother who raised her asks her to fire the mechanism consciously on a specific target.

Sofia was nine years old when she first killed someone without knowing it. Her parents had taken her to Dona Remedios, a curandera in Hidalgo, for a healing. Both her parents died that night. Dona Remedios took Sofia in and raised her as an assistant, a daughter, the center of her practice.

For twelve years, Sofia has attended every healing. She believed she was learning. What she was doing was working. The mechanism is not Dona Remedios's knowledge or faith -- it is Sofia's unconscious judgment. The illness moves through her. It finds a new host. Sofia has never known who pays the cost, or that there is a cost at all.

Now Dona Remedios is dying of terminal liver disease. She has spent twelve years learning the mechanism with clinical precision, understanding what Sofia does not: that the instrument can be aimed consciously at a specific target. All it requires is Sofia's willingness.

Dona Remedios asks Sofia to save her life. The request has a structure Sofia cannot process cleanly: the woman asking her to commit a deliberate murder is the only family she has ever known -- who raised her after Sofia's own unknowing mechanism killed her parents on the night of the healing that was supposed to save them. If Sofia refuses, Dona Remedios dies. If Sofia agrees, she becomes a conscious murderer and must choose who pays.

The horror of EL REMEDIO is that there is no path to innocence. Sofia never was innocent. She has been an instrument since she was nine. The film is about what she does with that knowledge -- and whether the woman who made her into an instrument deserves her mercy or her justice.

Comparable titles: Talk to Me , Hereditary , The Wailing

Talk to Me ($91.9M on a $4.5M budget) proved that global audiences will embrace rules-based ritual horror built around a specific transfer mechanism when the logic is airtight and the emotional stakes are personal. The curandera and faith healer world has no serious horror treatment at theatrical or streaming scale. LO QUE CARMEN PROMETIO (greenlighted) proves Lemon's horror pipeline is active and Billy has appetite for Mexican folk-horror at feature scale.

title: EL SEÑOR UNIVERSO (Mr. Universe)
logline: An out-of-shape 42-year-old accountant goes viral with 40 million views when filmed spectacularly failing at a public park workout -- and his employer wants to use him as the face of their new fitness campaign.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL SEÑOR UNIVERSO (Mr. Universe).
Format: Feature Film.

An out-of-shape 42-year-old accountant goes viral with 40 million views when filmed spectacularly failing at a public park workout -- and his employer wants to use him as the face of their new fitness campaign.

ROBERTO MORALES, 42, is an accountant who has not exercised intentionally since 2019. One Monday morning, inspired by a motivational post, he attempts a workout at a public park using equipment he misreads the instructions for. Someone films six minutes of spectacular, genuine failure accompanied by Roberto's cheerful muttering. By Wednesday Roberto has 40 million views and a nickname: El Señor Universo. His employer, a sports supplement company, wants to sign him as their anti-influencer spokesperson -- authentic failure as a brand value, bodies that don't look like bodies in ads. Roberto's ex-girlfriend NATALIA, a fitness instructor who left him because he never took care of himself, is being contracted by a competitor to expose the fraud. Roberto must navigate sudden fame, a campaign he never auditioned for, and a woman who knew him before anyone was watching.

Comparable titles: I Feel Pretty , Game Night , Drop Dead Gorgeous

Body-positive comedy resonates commercially and critically. The era of perfect fitness content is in active backlash. Authenticity as a brand value is commercially proven in 2026. Viral failure premise is culturally native to the current media landscape.

title: EL TERAPEUTA DE MI ESPOSA
logline: A checked-out Mexico City physiotherapist saves his wife's therapist from Russian mobsters by paying an 800,000-peso gambling debt -- on one condition: the therapist helps him win his wife back while letting him secretly listen to her sessions.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL TERAPEUTA DE MI ESPOSA.
Format: Feature Film.

A checked-out Mexico City physiotherapist saves his wife's therapist from Russian mobsters by paying an 800,000-peso gambling debt -- on one condition: the therapist helps him win his wife back while letting him secretly listen to her sessions.

Santiago (42) is a physiotherapist in Mexico City who has been treating the wrong leg of his patient Doña Tere for seventeen sessions. He is not a bad man. He is a distracted one. Present logistically, economically, physically -- but he stopped listening. To his patients. To his wife. To himself.

His wife Ximena (38) started therapy because the marriage has developed a hole she cannot name. Her therapist is Manuel. Santiago hears that name the way you hear a verdict.

The night Santiago goes to confront Manuel, he finds him dangling off a balcony. Boris -- a Russian mob boss, 54 years old, unexpectedly warm -- is about to drop him over an 800,000-peso gambling debt. Santiago, running on pure impulse, says he will pay. Boris gives him three days. Manuel, white with fear, agrees to two conditions: help Santiago win Ximena back, and let Santiago secretly listen to her sessions.

What follows is a comedy about a man who finally hears his wife through the wrong channel, for the wrong reasons, and still does not understand what she is saying. Santiago is a diagnostician by training. He processes the marriage crisis the way he processes a patient: anamnesis, treatment plan, protocol. He hears Ximena say she misses being asked small questions. He calls her that night and asks if she ate. He thinks that went well.

The real opponent is not Martín, Ximena's attentive ex who makes her feel seen. The real opponent is Santiago's certainty that he already knows the diagnosis. The cost of winning is that he has to stop being right and start being present.

The film ends with both of them painting their own house. A dog named Boris runs through the sala. Santiago asks Ximena what she wants for dinner. She notices that he is waiting for the answer.

Comparable titles: Recién Cazado  -- married-man-in-trouble, Mexican theatrical, direct market comp; Game Night  -- domestic comedy colliding with organized crime, same tonal register; Casi Familia  -- Mexican theatrical comedy with emotional weight and family stakes

Netflix Mexico has $1 billion committed (2025-2028) to locally produced content. Mexican domestic comedy that is both funny and true is consistently the strongest performer in the Latin American theatrical market -- Recién Cazado (2018), Hasta Que la Boda Nos Separe (2020), Casi Familia (2019). The therapy-stigma engine and the marriage-in-crisis premise are maximally current without being topical. The lane for character-rooted Mexican dark comedy in this exact space has been open since 2020.

title: EL TERMINADOR DE RELACIONES (The Relationship Terminator)
logline: Mexico City's most efficient professional breakup specialist is hired to torpedo a wedding, discovers the groom has hired a seduction coach to save it, and the two enemies fall for each other while running their worst plays on the same couple.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL TERMINADOR DE RELACIONES (The Relationship Terminator).
Format: Feature Film.

Mexico City's most efficient professional breakup specialist is hired to torpedo a wedding, discovers the groom has hired a seduction coach to save it, and the two enemies fall for each other while running their worst plays on the same couple.

Renata is an ex-litigator who runs Mexico City's premier clean-breakup service. Her clients call her La Terminadora. She gives other people the courage to leave because she watched her parents stay married and miserable for the kids, and swore she would always be the one who walks first. She is hired by a frightened bride to end a wedding quietly, two weeks out. Then she learns the groom has secretly hired Aldo, a celebrity seduction coach who sells men El Metodo, to save the same marriage. Aldo is her exact mirror. She severs love to stay safe, he counterfeits love to stay safe, and he is the one man who will not take the exit she offers. He names her plays out loud, tells her she is not freeing women but recruiting them, and then does the worse thing: he offers her the real version of the thing she sells as a fraud. As the proxy war escalates across the engagement party, the despedida, and the rehearsal dinner, Renata keeps gathering proof that this couple actually loves each other, which detonates the lie she built her life on. She wins the war and loses everything that mattered in it. At rock bottom she sees she is not her parents' opposite, she is their mirror: they stayed out of fear, she leaves out of fear. The climax is the inverse of her whole life. For once she does not run the exit. She stays, says the goodbye that means stay, and asks to be chosen.

Comparable titles: No Manches Frida  and Mesa de Regalos  for the domestic comedy benchmark; The Proposal and Liar Liar for the professional-whose-gift-fails-them engine; Intocable for the LOL-with-heart register.

The female-led Mexican theatrical comedy lane is open: Mexican films were five percent of the domestic theatrical audience in 2025 and comedy dominated that slate, with no dual-protagonist enemies-to-lovers comp in the market, so this is a format pioneer rather than a slot-filler. The expert-who-cannot-fix-their-own-life comedy is the dominant global register right now.

title: EL ÚNICO INOCENTE (The Only Innocent One)
logline: A timid notary public who witnesses a cartel hit is relocated to a small town and discovers every resident is hiding from something -- and he is the only genuinely innocent person there.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EL ÚNICO INOCENTE (The Only Innocent One).
Format: Feature Film.

A timid notary public who witnesses a cartel hit is relocated to a small town and discovers every resident is hiding from something -- and he is the only genuinely innocent person there.

Arturo Mendoza, 38, has spent his entire career notarizing documents he was never supposed to read carefully. He is precise, invisible, and constitutionally unable to lie. He witnesses a cartel execution at a parking garage in Polanco -- by accident, from the wrong angle, at the wrong moment -- and is immediately extracted by government handlers before the shooters can identify him.

He is relocated to San Jacinto, a small Queretaro town that, on paper, is a perfectly ordinary Mexican municipality. In practice, San Jacinto is an unofficial colony of people who have each been relocated there by someone, for different reasons, under different arrangements. The former cartel accountant is now the mayor. The school principal is a former forensic accountant who disappeared from a Hacienda investigation. The pharmacist is a woman whose testimony would have implicated half the Mexico City police union. Everyone in San Jacinto is hiding something. Everyone except Arturo.

The irony the title announces: in a town full of criminals, former criminals, and people who made compromises they cannot name, the one person without a secret is the most dangerous person there. Arturo keeps noticing things. He keeps asking questions. He does not understand that the town's survival depends on nobody noticing anything.

The opponent is the mayor, Rodrigo Fuentes -- former cartel accountant, genuinely beloved local leader, architect of every quiet arrangement that keeps San Jacinto functional. Rodrigo does not want to hurt Arturo. He wants Arturo to stop being Arturo. When that proves impossible, Rodrigo moves to sacrifice him -- and the climax requires Arturo to make a choice: expose Rodrigo and destroy the town's fragile equilibrium, or sacrifice himself to protect people who have spent weeks preparing to betray him.

Arturo sacrifices himself. That choice -- protecting people he knows will betray him because it is simply the right thing to do -- is the decision that reveals who he is and earns the comedy its emotional ending.

Comparable titles: Knives Out , The Whole Nine Yards , Hot Fuzz

The black comedy about institutional corruption and the lone honest person inside a dishonest system is having a global moment (Glass Onion, Knives Out, Hot Fuzz). Mexico's current political climate -- widespread cynicism about every institution paired with genuine hunger for characters who simply do the right thing -- makes a comedy about one genuinely innocent person navigating a town full of carefully-managed guilt feel urgent and cathartic.

title: EXAMEN FINAL (Final Exam)
logline: A control-freak PTA mom learns her daughter cheated into an elite high school through the same ring her oldest rival secretly runs.
cleanScript:
Pitch: EXAMEN FINAL (Final Exam).
Format: Feature Film.

A control-freak PTA mom learns her daughter cheated into an elite high school through the same ring her oldest rival secretly runs.

Carmen Garza, 47, has spent fifteen years performing the role of the right kind of mother: academic purity, no shortcuts, moral superiority over every other parent at the school gate. Her whole identity rests on it. What she wants is simple and total: for her daughter Sofia to ace the COMIPEMS placement exam and confirm, one more time, that Carmen did motherhood correctly. Then she discovers Sofia is already enrolled as a client in a professional cheating ring serving 200 elite families, run quietly for years by Patricia Salgado, Carmen's fifteen-year PTA nemesis. Patricia offers a clean trade: silence for silence. Because Carmen acts to protect Sofia rather than expose the truth, each move pulls her deeper into the machine she despises. She covers for one parent, then manages a second, then finds herself effectively helping run the operation to keep Sofia clear, becoming the thing her entire self-image was built against. At the exam certification ceremony, with one sentence able to bury Patricia and save face, Carmen instead chooses to confess publicly, detonating the spotless reputation she spent fifteen years building, because the only way to actually reach her daughter is to stop performing perfection at her. She loses the identity. She gets the kid. The comedy runs on competence becoming liability; the wound underneath is a daughter who cheated precisely because she could never be the version of herself Carmen needed her to be.

Comparable titles: Bad Moms ; ¿Que Culpa Tiene el Nino?

COMIPEMS placement-exam anxiety is a near-universal Mexican middle-class pressure point, and the cheating economy around elite-school admission is culturally live and immediately recognizable.

title: FALSA VIP (Fake VIP)
logline: A Polanco luxury mall's announcement of its VIP Customer of the Year triggers a four-hour reckoning when the woman named -- a daughter-in-law who has been secretly using her ruined suegra's membership for three years -- must survive a public ceremony alongside the suegra who cannot admit she lost everything, before the VIP Relations manager who's suspected fraud for three years figures it out.
cleanScript:
Pitch: FALSA VIP (Fake VIP).
Format: Feature Film.

A Polanco luxury mall's announcement of its VIP Customer of the Year triggers a four-hour reckoning when the woman named -- a daughter-in-law who has been secretly using her ruined suegra's membership for three years -- must survive a public ceremony alongside the suegra who cannot admit she lost everything, before the VIP Relations manager who's suspected fraud for three years figures it out.

ELENA VARGAS DE MONTOYA, 38, has spent twelve years mediating between her husband and his mother. She is so invisible in her own marriage that the only place she ever felt like a full person was the VIP lounge she was impersonating someone else to enter.

The membership belongs to SENORA CONSUELO MONTOYA-VIDAL, 67 -- her suegra. Three years ago, the suegra lost her fortune and could no longer maintain the membership. Rather than admit this to her social circle, she let Elena use the card. The suegra has not been inside this mall in three years.

When the mall announces SENORA MONTOYA-VIDAL as VIP Customer of the Year, LICENCIADA PATRICIA BERMUDEZ -- the VIP Relations Manager who built the program's integrity by hand over eighteen years and has suspected fraud on this account for three years -- pulls Elena aside: I need to verify your identity. Elena calls the suegra in a panic. The suegra arrives in a taxi in her last good dress. She and Patricia make eye contact across the lobby. Patricia's expression goes completely still.

The verification meeting. Patricia and the suegra conduct a twelve-year reunion in the language of people who know each other well enough to lie beautifully. The suegra improvises: she sent Elena in her place due to a health matter. Elena watches the suegra be completely magnificent while making everything worse. The ceremony requires the VIP Customer of the Year to answer, on stage, questions about her relationship to the brand, her most meaningful purchase, her vision for the future of Mexican luxury. The suegra has no idea what Elena has been doing in her name for three years. They are handed the script.

Elena's husband arrives -- the mall sent him a surprise VIP guest invitation. He has never heard of his mother's VIP membership. He sits in the front row. The suegra is on stage. The interview begins. The suegra looks at Elena in the front row. Elena looks at her husband. Her husband looks at his mother. Patricia opens a folder. Elena stays in her seat. She nods once. The suegra only picks up the microphone because Elena decided she could.

The comedy's engine is the suegra: she cannot admit she is ruined, so the daughter-in-law she has spent years ignoring is the only person who can save the family status -- and the suegra has to let her.

Comparable titles: Mesa de Regalos , Nosotros los Nobles , Ahora o Nunca

Mesa de Regalos (2025, 1.9M Mexican theatrical admissions) proved the market for women-led class-collision comedy. The suegra/daughter-in-law dynamic is at peak cultural resonance in Mexican popular entertainment.

title: HORA MEXICANA (Mexican Time)
logline: When Mexico's most chronically late man inherits a legendary watch company and has 72 hours and five formal meetings to close a deal with German buyers, he keeps losing every metric and winning every room -- until his cousin reveals the one thing that actually matters, and Juan Carlos must decide whether the clock he has been running from was the wrong clock all along.
cleanScript:
Pitch: HORA MEXICANA (Mexican Time).
Format: Feature Film.

When Mexico's most chronically late man inherits a legendary watch company and has 72 hours and five formal meetings to close a deal with German buyers, he keeps losing every metric and winning every room -- until his cousin reveals the one thing that actually matters, and Juan Carlos must decide whether the clock he has been running from was the wrong clock all along.

JUAN CARLOS GARCIA MARTINEZ, late 40s, has never been on time for anything in his adult life -- not because he is reckless but because he is a Type 9 Peacemaker who uses lateness as self-erasure: arriving late means no one is counting on him to deliver an outcome he might fail. When his grandfather's century-old watchmaking company passes to him via inheritance -- bypassing COUSIN RODRIGO VARGAS, who managed it with German-style efficiency for 18 months and believes he earned it -- Juan Carlos has 72 hours and five formal business sessions to impress German buyers or lose the company.

The irony that drives every scene: the Germans are not buying a precision timepiece company. They are buying the philosophy that makes a watch mean something. And the only man who carries that philosophy is the one who has never once been on time. Each session escalates the battle between Rodrigo's metrics and Juan Carlos's incurable warmth. In Session 1, Juan Carlos arrives 22 minutes late and accidentally starts a personal friendship with the German CEO, who spent a year in Mexico City in 1991 and immediately recognizes something he has been trying to find in 18 months of formal management.

Threading through Sessions 2-4: Juan Carlos's estranged daughter, whose call Rodrigo engineers to collide with Session 3. Juan Carlos takes the call. The Germans wait 30 minutes. The CEO asks why. Juan Carlos tells the truth. The CEO says: 'Your grandfather would have done the same thing.'

The film earns its emotional turn on the factory floor, where Juan Carlos sits with DON AURELIO, the 74-year-old master artisan, watching him work in silence for two hours. He returns and hands the CEO the grandfather's journal, open to one paragraph: 'A watch that makes you anxious about time is a device. A watch that makes you wonder why time matters is something else entirely.' The CEO reads it. He asks when they can sign.

Comparable titles: Nosotros los Nobles , Que Culpa Tiene el Niño , La Convención

Mexico's cultural identity is having a global moment, and 'hora mexicana' is one of the country's most beloved and immediately legible idioms. The 2026 global conversation about authenticity vs. efficiency -- in workplaces, in relationships, in national identity -- gives this premise immediate relevance across all markets.

title: INAMOVIBLE (Immovable)
logline: When Mexico City's most feared mall property manager -- 400 evictions, never lost a case -- is assigned to clear a 94-year-old kiosk owner whose 1978 lease is bulletproof, he deploys every institutional weapon he has and watches each one turn her into the most famous abuela in Mexico.
cleanScript:
Pitch: INAMOVIBLE (Immovable).
Format: Feature Film.

When Mexico City's most feared mall property manager -- 400 evictions, never lost a case -- is assigned to clear a 94-year-old kiosk owner whose 1978 lease is bulletproof, he deploys every institutional weapon he has and watches each one turn her into the most famous abuela in Mexico.

RODRIGO TORRES, 42, has made a career of clearing the way. Four hundred evictions. He has never lost a case. His wound: his mother worked a market kiosk for thirty years in Tepito, and he spent his career demolishing the world she occupied, believing he was escaping it. He never admitted that to himself.

His assignment: clear DONA LUPE, 94, from her corner kiosk in the lobby of a new Polanco mega-mall. She has occupied that corner since 1978. Her lease -- reviewed by three law firms -- is bulletproof. She sells hand-embroidered napkins, tchotchkes, and her attention. She has survived fourteen previous eviction attempts by four different mall owners.

Rodrigo's first move: health inspection. He files building code complaints. The city inspector arrives. He is her nephew. Her kiosk is in perfect compliance. The Starbucks gets fined. Rodrigo gets a bill.

Rodrigo's second move: make her famous. His PR team commissions a charming heritage profile piece on Dona Lupe, betting she'll go viral, feel grateful, and accept a generous buyout as a graceful exit. She goes viral in nine days. Four million Instagram followers. The buyout offers arrive: Mercado Libre, Oxxo, Pemex all want to co-brand with her. She declines all of them. Rodrigo's boss calls from Madrid: "You made her famous." "I didn't mean to." "Doesn't matter."

Rodrigo's third move: bring in the corporate executives for the renovation announcement. The Madrid partners fly in for a site tour. Dona Lupe has invited all 4 million followers to a Kiosk Appreciation Day that same morning. The executives' SUVs navigate 3,000 people in the parking structure. When they reach the lobby, the mall's most feared property manager is standing in the middle of the largest commercial event his building has ever hosted -- organized by the 94-year-old woman the renovation is supposed to replace. She waves at him from behind her kiosk.

Then the Madrid executive asks Rodrigo: "Is she under contract?" Rodrigo has the eviction notice face-down in his jacket pocket. He says: "No." The executive says: "Renovate around her. The kiosk stays -- on her terms." Rodrigo's boss looks at him. Two days later: Rodrigo is fired.

Dona Lupe sees exactly what he was doing. She saw it from the first day. She is the reason he cannot finish this job -- and she is the closest thing to his mother he has ever let himself be near.

Comparable titles: Nosotros los Nobles , Que Culpa Tiene el Nino? , Gran Torino

Gentrification and displacement of working-class vendors from public spaces is a live cultural conversation in Mexico City in 2026. The anti-gentrification comedy with a 94-year-old national-abuela protagonist taps directly into that tension with warmth rather than politics.

title: LA COSECHA (The Harvest)
logline: A CDMX transplant surgeon discovers her rural colleague has harvested organs from living patients for twenty years under a supernatural pact -- and her patients are now speaking in voices not their own.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA COSECHA (The Harvest).
Format: Feature Film.

A CDMX transplant surgeon discovers her rural colleague has harvested organs from living patients for twenty years under a supernatural pact -- and her patients are now speaking in voices not their own.

A transplant surgeon in CDMX begins noticing something wrong in her recovery ward. Her patients are saying things they could not know. Speaking in voices. Remembering places they have never been. She traces the organs to a rural hospital in Michoacan -- the hospital run by her colleague and trusted friend Dr. Valeria Nunez.

Nunez has been harvesting organs from patients who are marginally alive for twenty years. The arrangement is not with the hospital administration. The arrangement predates the hospital. Longevity in exchange for consciousness delivered at the threshold of death. Nunez looks forty-three. She is sixty-eight.

The surgeon cannot report without losing her license for accepting the organs without due diligence. Her daughter has been visiting the rural hospital with her for years and has begun exhibiting the fragments' behavior. The horror is not the supernatural mechanic -- it is the institutional chain that made the surgeon complicit before she ever understood what she was part of.

Comparable titles: Hereditary , Get Out , Talk to Me

Healthcare system horror is the genre's fastest-growing institutional lane post-pandemic. The Mexican rural-urban healthcare split is a real and documented wound. No horror film has used organ transplant complicity as its engine at this scale.

title: LA ENTREGA (The Delivery)
logline: A veteran midwife who falsified a stillbirth certificate and disposed of a living infant thirty years ago faces the entity she set in motion when it returns -- through her own unknowing adult daughter -- to collect what was promised.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA ENTREGA (The Delivery).
Format: Feature Film.

A veteran midwife who falsified a stillbirth certificate and disposed of a living infant thirty years ago faces the entity she set in motion when it returns -- through her own unknowing adult daughter -- to collect what was promised.

Consuelo Mendoza has delivered over three thousand babies in her 40-year career. She is revered, trusted, beloved. What no one knows is that thirty years ago she falsified a stillbirth certificate and disposed of a living infant -- the illegitimate child of a powerful family who paid her to make the problem disappear. She built her life above that secret like a house above a fault line.

When a young woman named Mireya arrives at Consuelo's clinic seeking prenatal care, Consuelo feels an inexplicable pull -- a recognition she cannot explain. What unfolds is the most precise horror imaginable: Mireya is the woman that infant became, returned to Consuelo not by design but as an instrument of the entity Consuelo's crime activated thirty years ago. Mireya has no agenda. She is warm, trusting, grateful. She thinks Consuelo is a gift.

The entity works through Mireya's presence, proximity, and love -- tightening its arrangement around Consuelo with every appointment. The cost of escape is naming the crime. The cost of silence is Mireya's life and Consuelo's soul. The film's horror is not the monster arriving from outside -- it is the monster Consuelo built with her own hands, returned to her through the face of the person she must simultaneously love and destroy.

Comparable titles: The Orphanage , Hereditary , Huesera

Maternal horror is the defining prestige lane of this decade (Hereditary, The Babadook, Huesera). This story takes the engine to its most precise formulation: the midwife who is the source of the horror. Netflix LatAm has active appetite for culturally specific Mexican horror with female protagonists at the center.

title: LA HIJA RENTADA (The Rented Daughter)
logline: A woman who ghosted her family for twenty years comes home to find a professional substitute has been living as her for seven years -- and the fake version is objectively better.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA HIJA RENTADA (The Rented Daughter).
Format: Feature Film.

A woman who ghosted her family for twenty years comes home to find a professional substitute has been living as her for seven years -- and the fake version is objectively better.

Daniela Soto, 40, ghosted her family in Guadalajara two decades ago. She returns expecting resentment and silence. She finds Claudia, 35, a professional family attendant who has attended every birthday, every hospital visit, every Christmas, every quinceañera -- in Daniela's place, as Daniela -- for seven years. The family does not know Claudia is not her. They just think she finally came around.

Claudia is objectively better at being Daniela than Daniela was. She remembered the nieces' names. She showed up when their grandmother died. She sat with Daniela's mother through chemotherapy appointments Daniela did not attend. The family loves the person they think is Daniela.

Claudia has a livelihood and an identity at stake. She built this life. She is good at it. If the original returns, Claudia loses everything she spent seven years constructing. Daniela must now decide whether she has come home to reclaim her family or to be confronted with twenty years of evidence that she does not deserve them -- by the stranger who did the work she refused to do.

Comparable titles: The Certification , Nosotros los Nobles , Four Weddings and a Funeral

The professional-substitute comedy format has no entry in the Mexican market. The premise is structurally distinct from any existing family comedy: it does not ask 'can she re-integrate the family?' It asks 'does she have the right to displace someone who actually showed up?' Netflix LatAm theatrical comedy is an open lane.

title: LA PROMETIDA (The Promised Bride)
logline: A bride discovers her grandmother's 200-year family blessing was an address to a supernatural entity that arrives at the reception to claim her.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA PROMETIDA (The Promised Bride).
Format: Feature Film.

A bride discovers her grandmother's 200-year family blessing was an address to a supernatural entity that arrives at the reception to claim her.

On her wedding day, a bride receives the blessing her grandmother has passed down through every generation for two centuries. Within hours she understands: it was never a blessing. It is a spoken formula that delivers one bride per century to a supernatural entity in exchange for protecting every other woman in the family line. The entity has arrived at the reception.

The grandmother is present. She knew. She made the same choice her grandmother made: sacrifice one to protect all. She is not a villain -- she is a woman who loves her family and has decided this is an acceptable cost. She will not undo it without being broken in a way she has not allowed herself to be broken before.

The bride's choices collapse rapidly. If she runs, the entity takes her husband in her place. If she stays, she honors the formula. If she confronts her grandmother, she must destroy the relationship that raised her and sever the protection of every other woman in her bloodline. One night. One reception venue. No clean exit. The horror is not the entity -- it is the woman who loves her and did this anyway.

Comparable titles: Hereditary , Midsommar , The Conjuring

Talk to Me (2023) proved ritual-contact horror at $91.9M on a $4.5M budget. No Mexican wedding has been used as a horror engine at theatrical or streaming scale. Netflix LatAm has active horror appetite. Natural global release window exists in the bridal-season market.

title: LA RECETA (The Recipe)
logline: The founder of Mexico's most beloved taco empire discovers the Oaxacan woman whose recipe built his fortune has been working his prep station for 20 years -- without knowing why.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA RECETA (The Recipe).
Format: Feature Film.

The founder of Mexico's most beloved taco empire discovers the Oaxacan woman whose recipe built his fortune has been working his prep station for 20 years -- without knowing why.

CARLOS SANDOVAL, 51, built EL TACO DEL ABUELO into a 40-location national chain, a TV show, and a 180,000-copy memoir -- all on a family recipe he has been telling the same story about for 20 years. The story is this: his grandfather, a Jalisco farmer, developed the mole blend in 1961. He passed it to Carlos. Carlos built an empire. The true story is this: in 1961, Carlos's grandfather paid DOÑA REMEDIOS VÁSQUEZ, an Oaxacan cook, 300 pesos for the right to use her family recipe card. She kept a copy. She never heard from anyone again. Twenty years ago, Doña Remedios, now 80, took a job as a prep worker at EL TACO DEL ABUELO's original location. She works his morning prep station. She has never connected the recipe on the laminated cards to the one in her mother's notebook. Until a journalist asks Carlos to make the mole on camera, from memory, and he cannot. LA RECETA is a dark comedy about cultural appropriation, culinary mythology, and the moment an empire meets the woman it was built on.

Comparable titles: The Menu , Matando Cabos , Como Agua para Chocolate -- dark comedy register with culinary and class stakes

Cultural appropriation and culinary identity are live public conversations in Mexico in 2026. The Oaxacan food origin story has real teeth that no international studio can render with authority. Dark comedy with social commentary is the dominant streaming register globally.

title: LA SEGUNDA PIEL (The Second Skin)
logline: An archaeologist excavating beneath Cholula's colonial church finds sealed chambers bearing her own family's names -- and must decide whether to accept a 500-year guarding obligation or destroy the codices and allow the containment to fail permanently.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA SEGUNDA PIEL (The Second Skin).
Format: Feature Film.

An archaeologist excavating beneath Cholula's colonial church finds sealed chambers bearing her own family's names -- and must decide whether to accept a 500-year guarding obligation or destroy the codices and allow the containment to fail permanently.

An archaeologist on a routine excavation beneath Cholula's colonial church -- built directly on top of a pre-Hispanic pyramid -- breaks through into a sealed chamber that was not in any record. The chamber walls are covered in colonial-era codices, and the family names inscribed on them are hers. A 72-year-old family elder arrives within hours of the discovery, warned by the entity inside before the archaeologist knew what she had found. The elder has one message: the family has been the chamber's guardians for 500 years, the obligation passes to the next generation, and refusing it does not end the obligation -- it breaks the containment. The horror engine is the scholar confronting the limit of her discipline: the codices are real, the entity is real, and every test she runs confirms what she has spent her career dismissing. Her scientific framework is not being disproven -- it simply has no category for what she has found. The film's middle tracks the archaeologist applying her full intellectual capacity to the problem, while the entity makes clear through escalating events that it has been waiting for exactly this person to be standing in this chamber. The climax forces a binary: accept the guarding obligation (destroy the career and the worldview that holds her identity together) or destroy the codices and release an entity that has been sealed for five centuries. The highest-scoring concept in this batch (81.1/100). Cholula's church-on-pyramid setting is one of the most commercially distinctive horror locations in Mexico.

Comparable titles: Hereditary , The Ritual , La Llorona

Archaeological horror with indigenous colonial history is an open lane in Spanish-language genre content. The Cholula setting -- a colonial church built on top of a pyramid, a real and documented site -- gives this film a visual premise that requires no explanation to international audiences.

title: LA TONA (The Spirit Double)
logline: A Zapotec domestic worker who has unknowingly carried her employer's bloodline entity for 20 years discovers the entity is transferring to the child -- and she cannot leave to stop it because her employer holds her documents.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA TONA (The Spirit Double).
Format: Feature Film.

A Zapotec domestic worker who has unknowingly carried her employer's bloodline entity for 20 years discovers the entity is transferring to the child -- and she cannot leave to stop it because her employer holds her documents.

Isabel has worked in the Montes household for 20 years. She is undocumented, her papers are held by Elena Montes, and she has no leverage to leave. What she has never been told: she was born a tona -- a Zapotec lineage spirit vessel -- and the entity that was born with her has been the unseen protector of the Montes bloodline for those 20 years.

Now the entity is transferring. It has found Elena's adopted son Marco as its next bloodline host -- because Marco is, biologically, from the same Zapotec lineage Isabel is from. Isabel is the carrier. Marco is the heir. The transfer will consume the child's own identity, replacing it with the entity's ancient framework. Isabel must stop it.

She cannot leave to get help -- Elena will call immigration. She cannot explain what she knows without revealing she has been the supernatural carrier living in the house. Elena Montes is not a villain; she is a woman in a crisis she cannot see, and her every protective instinct (keep Isabel close, monitor Marco, restrict access) accelerates the transfer. The horror is a cage made of exploitation and care in equal measure.

The reversal requires Isabel to summon the entity back into herself permanently -- to become the vessel forever, not just the carrier. She walks free. Marco survives. She carries it the rest of her life. That is the choice, and she makes it.

Comparable titles: The Wailing ; Hereditary ; Roma

Oaxacan and Zapotec cultural specificity is achieving global visibility. Mexico City domestic worker exploitation is a live political and legal issue. The tona concept -- a Mesoamerican companion spirit born with a person at birth -- has no horror treatment at any scale, theatrical or streaming.

title: LA VISITA (The Visit)
logline: On her first overnight shift at a rural Puebla hospital, a nurse notices the same visitor signing in under different names while patients on the fourth floor die of natural causes -- and the moment she understands what she is looking at, she becomes the next thing on the visitor's list.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA VISITA (The Visit).
Format: Feature Film.

On her first overnight shift at a rural Puebla hospital, a nurse notices the same visitor signing in under different names while patients on the fourth floor die of natural causes -- and the moment she understands what she is looking at, she becomes the next thing on the visitor's list.

Lupita is a newly graduated nurse, first overnight shift at a remote government hospital in rural Puebla. The shift is quiet. She is told to stay at the nursing station and not bother the fourth floor attending. She does not understand yet that the fourth floor is not a hospital ward -- it is a mechanism.

The visitor has been coming for months, signing in under a different name each time, staying forty minutes, leaving before anyone asks questions. The attending doctor has been clearing the death certificates because the visitor holds leverage he cannot escape. Lupita, who does not know the system, does not understand she is not supposed to look at the visitor board. She looks.

The horror unfolds in real time: what she thought was a malfunctioning hospital is a systematically maintained killing operation, and the moment she realizes this she becomes the next thing on the visitor's list. The film is a closed-space night-terror: one building, one night, one woman who knows too much and cannot leave without proof.

The institutional villain -- the doctor who enables it -- is not evil by nature but by accumulated compliance, which makes him more terrifying than any monster because he is always the rational voice explaining why Lupita is making a mistake.

Comparable titles: Don't Breathe , The Autopsy of Jane Doe , Misery

Closed-space horror with institutional complicity has no equivalent in Mexican cinema. The rural hospital setting is specific to a real Mexican healthcare experience -- the overnight shift at an understaffed provincial facility is immediately culturally legible. Micro-budget theatrical horror.

title: LLORAR DE OFICIO (Crying by Trade)
logline: When Mexico's most conflict-avoidant llorador discovers he has been hired for the funeral of the man who destroyed his family, he must maintain professional cover for three hours while the dead man's suspicious son interrogates him -- and the only tool he has for every evasion is the one that made him available for this funeral in the first place.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LLORAR DE OFICIO (Crying by Trade).
Format: Feature Film.

When Mexico's most conflict-avoidant llorador discovers he has been hired for the funeral of the man who destroyed his family, he must maintain professional cover for three hours while the dead man's suspicious son interrogates him -- and the only tool he has for every evasion is the one that made him available for this funeral in the first place.

MARCO PEREZ is Mexico's most technically consistent llorador -- a hired mourner who built a career across Oaxaca, Veracruz, and Michoacán attending 400 strangers' funerals because standing near other people's grief gave him access to emotion without requiring him to own any of it. He is a Type 9 Peacemaker who says 'no hay problema' when there is a problem, and agrees with things he knows to be false to keep any room comfortable. His professional success is a function of his wound.

The funeral he is hired to work is for DON AURELIO VAZQUEZ. When Marco walks through the door, he recognizes whose funeral it is. Don Aurelio destroyed Marco's family twenty years ago.

Marco must maintain llorador cover for three hours in a room full of the man's devoted community, while DON AURELIO's eldest son RODRIGO VAZQUEZ -- a Type 8 Challenger running the funeral with the restrained fury of a man who knows something is wrong and cannot name it -- corners Marco again and again in escalating corridor interrogations. Each invented story Marco tells to explain his presence must be more specific and more obviously false than the last. The comedy engine is structural: every communal praise of Don Aurelio forces Marco to choose between llorador professionalism (absorb, reflect, affirm) and twenty years of knowledge about who the man actually was.

The microphone climax: Rodrigo announces that Marco -- Don Aurelio's 'old friend who clearly knew him well' -- will be the final speaker. Marco walks up. He starts with avoidance. He does not stop. He covers everything. He cries without any technique at all. The priest tries to interrupt twice. Rodrigo unfolds his arms. When Marco finishes, Rodrigo is the first person to start clapping.

Comparable titles: No Se Aceptan Devoluciones , The Descendants , Bienvenue chez les Ch'tis

The llorador tradition is a real and nationally recognized institution across Oaxaca, Veracruz, and Michoacán.
