In 2026, as Mexico processes new cycles of collective grief alongside growing conversations about emotional labor and emotional avoidance, a dark comedy built around professional mourning arrives at precisely the right cultural moment.

title: LOS EXVOTOS (The Votive Offerings)
logline: An art restorer hired to catalog ex-votos at a Guanajuato pilgrimage shrine discovers hundreds of the paintings depict miracles not yet given -- painted by people who do not exist yet -- including one for the miracle that kept her alive at age seven.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LOS EXVOTOS (The Votive Offerings).
Format: Feature Film.

An art restorer hired to catalog ex-votos at a Guanajuato pilgrimage shrine discovers hundreds of the paintings depict miracles not yet given -- painted by people who do not exist yet -- including one for the miracle that kept her alive at age seven.

Valentina Reyes has spent fifteen years refusing to think about the car accident that killed her mother when she was seven. She survived. Her mother did not. The church she was taken to afterward -- a pilgrimage shrine outside Guanajuato -- is where she has now been hired to catalog a trove of ex-votos: the small painted offerings Mexicans have brought to holy sites for centuries to document miracles received.

But hundreds of the paintings are wrong. They depict scenes that have not happened yet -- people in contemporary clothes who do not yet exist, painted in the style of 19th-century folk offering. Each catalog entry she completes reveals more of the system: a miracle-credit ledger administered by something that has been operating in this shrine for longer than the shrine itself.

In a back case, wrapped in wax cloth, is an ex-voto dated 1989: a seven-year-old girl in a car accident, depicted alive. Her mother painted in the background, turning away.

Dona Esperanza, the shrine guardian, is not alarmed. She has administered the miracle-credit system for decades. The entity does not operate outside its rules. Valentina is not the one who owes the debt -- but she is what was promised. The entity does not cancel miracles. It adjusts the ledger.

What follows is a horror film about the cost of surviving something you were not supposed to survive, and what the supernatural does with a debt that has gone unpaid for fifteen years. Each ex-voto Valentina catalogs narrows her options. The painting that depicts her miracle is the only one in the shrine that has not yet been completed -- and the entity is patient, but not indefinitely.

Comparable titles: The Babadook , The Others , The Wailing

Mexican Catholic popular religion and the manda tradition are undergoing cultural reclamation -- ex-votos appear in global art exhibitions and their horror potential as painted evidence of unpaid supernatural debts is entirely unexplored at commercial scale.

title: LOS VEINTISÉIS (The Twenty-Six)
logline: A Mexico City Metro Line 12 maintenance worker who buried the structural warnings that killed 26 people is hunted by the embedded dead -- who will collapse the rebuilt infrastructure again unless he confesses.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LOS VEINTISÉIS (The Twenty-Six).
Format: Feature Film.

A Mexico City Metro Line 12 maintenance worker who buried the structural warnings that killed 26 people is hunted by the embedded dead -- who will collapse the rebuilt infrastructure again unless he confesses.

Ernesto survived the 2021 Línea 12 collapse because he suppressed his own safety reports. He knew the columns were failing. He filed the paperwork, then buried it when his supervisor threatened his job. Twenty-six people died in the overpass collapse. Ernesto kept his position, kept his family fed, and told himself the reports would have been ignored anyway.

Now the rebuilt infrastructure is failing again -- and only at night, only on Ernesto's shift. The 26 dead are inside the new concrete. They corrode the supports he inspects, trigger fail-safes from inside sealed tunnels, and force him to stay underground long after his shift ends. They are not trying to kill him. They are trying to make him stay until he admits what he buried. His contractor-plant supervisor is simultaneously engineering a second catastrophe and constructing a paper trail that puts Ernesto at the center of the new failure.

Every night Ernesto stays silent, the dead tighten. Every report he files honestly shrinks his family's security. The cost of confession is identical to the cost of his original deal: his job, his income, and the safety of the family he made the first lie to protect. The horror is that the moral calculus hasn't changed -- only the deadline.

Comparable titles: As Above So Below ; Sinister ; The Taking of Deborah Logan

The 2021 Línea 12 collapse remains unresolved -- no one has been prosecuted. In 2025 the Fiscalia confirmed systemic construction corruption. The civic wound is live and Mexican audiences have been waiting for reckoning that the courts haven't delivered.

title: MATERIAS PENDIENTES (Unfinished Business)
logline: A broke, failing grad student accepts a premium tutoring gig and discovers her client is a 44-year-old man who has been secretly retaking high school for 27 years.
cleanScript:
Pitch: MATERIAS PENDIENTES (Unfinished Business).
Format: Feature Film.

A broke, failing grad student accepts a premium tutoring gig and discovers her client is a 44-year-old man who has been secretly retaking high school for 27 years.

SOFIA LEAL, 27, is three years behind on her thesis, three months behind on rent, and one missed payment away from losing her library desk. When she accepts a premium tutoring gig through a cash-only app, she expects a struggling teenager. She finds GERARDO VILLANUEVA -- 44, wealthy, sitting at a small desk in the back of a room perfectly sized for a 15-year-old. Gerardo has been retaking high school for 27 years -- not because he is failing, but because he cannot move past the year his daughter was born and he made a choice he has never forgiven himself for. He keeps repeating the year to see if the outcome changes. Sofia came to teach algebra. She is now the only person who has ever asked him why he really comes back. Two people with unfinished business, one classroom, and a year that one of them needs to finally end.

Comparable titles: Billy Madison , Little Miss Sunshine , About Schmidt

Intergenerational comedies with unexpected emotional depth perform strongly across all age demographics. The premise is instantly understandable across education levels and cultures. Billy-approved register: warm, specific, funny with a real emotional engine underneath.

title: NO ME DIGAS JEFE (Don't Call Me Boss)
logline: A woman's mortifying one-night stand the night before her dream job's first day turns out to be her new boss -- and neither can admit it because both signed no-relationship policies.
cleanScript:
Pitch: NO ME DIGAS JEFE (Don't Call Me Boss).
Format: Feature Film.

A woman's mortifying one-night stand the night before her dream job's first day turns out to be her new boss -- and neither can admit it because both signed no-relationship policies.

LUISA MONTOYA, 31, is starting Monday as creative director at the agency she has been trying to get into for four years. Sunday night, she has a spectacular and completely uncharacteristic one-night stand with a man she knows only as Tomas: phone battery dead, no last name exchanged, gone before morning. Monday morning at her new desk: Tomas is the CEO she has never met. Both signed a no-relationship policy as a condition of their contracts. Both need this job. The chemistry refuses to disappear. The competition to pretend nothing happened -- while the work that brought them to the same company keeps throwing them together -- escalates from awkward to catastrophic. The proposal they are both hiding is professional, not personal: but only one of them knows that.

Comparable titles: The Proposal , Horrible Bosses , Bridesmaids

Workplace romantic comedy has found new commercial life in the post-2020 landscape where professional norms are extremely legible and therefore extremely comic. The no-relationship-policy premise is freshly relevant. No Spanish-language entry in this exact lane currently.

title: SANANDO AL SANADOR (Healing the Healer)
logline: A fraudulent self-help guru is forced to spend 30 days at his own retreat living by his own advice -- surrounded by the people whose lives he destroyed following it.
cleanScript:
Pitch: SANANDO AL SANADOR (Healing the Healer).
Format: Feature Film.

A fraudulent self-help guru is forced to spend 30 days at his own retreat living by his own advice -- surrounded by the people whose lives he destroyed following it.

SEBASTIÁN MORA, 42, built a $40M wellness empire on advice he has never followed, a retreat he has never visited, and a persona entirely invented. His books sell 800,000 copies per year. When a documentary crew gets too close and his publicist threatens to drop him, Sebastián is forced into his own 30-day retreat incognito. The cohort waiting for him: PATRICIA, who quit her job following his "follow your passion" chapter; BETO, who left his family to "find himself" using Sebastián's 5-step framework; and MARINA, the documentary host, undercover in the cohort. Every tool Sebastián uses to perform authenticity is now his cage. The comedy is the gap between the persona and the man -- but the tragedy, slowly, is that the gap is closing.

Comparable titles: Wild , The Certification , I Feel Pretty

Wellness culture backlash is a confirmed top-10 cultural trend for 2026 (MI validated). The fraudulent guru is the perfect comedic protagonist for an audience saturated with authentic-sounding nonsense. The Certification (Tepoztlan wellness satire) was approved by Billy -- this is the bolder theatrical version.

title: SE LE FUE LA MANO (She Went Too Far)
logline: When a rule-following notaría official accidentally transfers an entire real estate portfolio into her own name, she has 48 hours to reverse it before her boss -- the developer who now legally owes her everything -- destroys her career to get it back.
cleanScript:
Pitch: SE LE FUE LA MANO (She Went Too Far).
Format: Feature Film.

When a rule-following notaría official accidentally transfers an entire real estate portfolio into her own name, she has 48 hours to reverse it before her boss -- the developer who now legally owes her everything -- destroys her career to get it back.

Sandra is a single mother who built her entire professional identity on being the person who never makes mistakes with paperwork. She is a notaría official in Mexico City -- the kind of professional who processes other people's life decisions with perfect legal precision. She has never owned more than her small apartment.

On a routine late-night filing, a systems error combined with her countersignature transfers a mid-size real estate developer's entire portfolio -- twelve properties, dozens of pending transactions -- into her name. By morning, she legally owns everything her boss Eduardo has built over 20 years.

The error is technically reversible, but only if every affected party agrees to sign a correction within 48 hours. Eduardo has already consulted lawyers. The legal path back requires Sandra's cooperation, but the economic path forward requires him to discredit her completely. He calls in every favor with the local notaría authority, triggers a regulatory audit, and freezes her professional license.

Sandra has 48 hours. She cannot prove the error without her boss's cooperation, and her boss will never cooperate while she holds what he wants. She must use the legal precision that defined her career to navigate the one situation it was never designed to handle: owning what she was not supposed to, against the will of the person who should own it, while protecting her children's stability from a man who can take everything she has built.

The comedy lives in the collision between Sandra's compulsive rule-following and a situation where every rule she invokes creates the next complication.

Comparable titles: Mesa de Regalos  -- working-class protagonist navigating elite institutional machinery. PRESIDENTE POR ACCIDENTE  -- accidental authority as comedy engine. No me hables  -- female-led comedy with professional irony as the central engine.

Mexican class-and-bureaucracy comedy is having a theatrical moment (Mesa de Regalos, 1.7M admissions, 2025). The legal-bureaucratic comedy lane for women -- particularly working-class women fighting institutional machinery -- has no current incumbent in LatAm theatrical. Post-pandemic working-class protagonist comedies are the dominant comfort genre in Mexican streaming.

title: SU PROPIA MEDICINA (A Taste of His Own Medicine)
logline: Mexico's most famous marriage counselor is served divorce papers mid-press-tour by a wife who followed his own Step 7, and has 48 hours to save his live TV special while confronting every lie his career was built on.
cleanScript:
Pitch: SU PROPIA MEDICINA (A Taste of His Own Medicine).
Format: Feature Film.

Mexico's most famous marriage counselor is served divorce papers mid-press-tour by a wife who followed his own Step 7, and has 48 hours to save his live TV special while confronting every lie his career was built on.

Jorge Medina has saved 3,000 marriages, built a 1.2M-follower empire on his 9-Step Method, and is 48 hours from the live TV special that will make him Mexico's celebrity marriage authority. Then his wife Lucía serves him divorce papers using his own Step 7 -- methodically, correctly, and with documentation. She didn't just leave him. She followed his instructions. The irony is the engine: the man who diagnosed everyone else's marriage spent years refusing to look at his own. Jorge now faces the press tour while knowing his wife is gone, his brand is built on a foundation he didn't live, and the TV cameras go on regardless. Every interview forces him to speak his own words back to himself. Every audience question becomes an indictment. His producer needs him functional; his publicist needs him performing; the therapist he never listened to was right about everything. The film earns its comedy because Jorge is not a villain -- he genuinely believed what he taught. The lie was never conscious; it was the gap between the method and the man. To reach the third act, he must apply Step 7 to himself: acknowledge the cost of his professional dishonesty before an audience of 2 million, and lose the brand in order to keep his integrity. The cost is total and irreversible: 3,000 marriages, 4 books, the TV special, and the identity built on fixing what he failed to live. What he gets, if he pays it, is his first honest conversation.

Comparable titles: Liar Liar , Nosotros Los Nobles , About a Boy

Celebrity expert culture is collapsing in LatAm -- influencer trust dropped 58% to 38% between 2022 and 2026. The marriage counselor as failed practitioner is the exact cultural figure audiences are ready to laugh at and root for simultaneously.

title: SUELTAME, MA (Let Me Go, Mom)
logline: A therapist who built a career teaching parents to release adult children discovers his controlling mother enrolled in his workshop -- front row, every week.
cleanScript:
Pitch: SUELTAME, MA (Let Me Go, Mom).
Format: Feature Film.

A therapist who built a career teaching parents to release adult children discovers his controlling mother enrolled in his workshop -- front row, every week.

Mauricio Sandoval, 38, spent sixteen years building a therapy practice and a six-week workshop called Sueltame, Ma -- designed to teach parents to stop controlling their adult children. He has helped two hundred families. He has never told his mother it exists.

Graciela has found it. She is enrolled. Front row. Week one. She does not know this is her son's practice, because Mauricio uses a professional alias.

For six weeks, Mauricio must run his workshop on stage while his mother sits in the front row and performs the exact controlling behaviors he is teaching other parents to unlearn -- without either of them acknowledging the other in front of fourteen strangers. If Mauricio reveals himself, he loses his professional credibility and the alias that protects his private life. If he stays silent, he sits through his mother's weekly performance of the thing he has spent sixteen years treating, and gets no closer to the acknowledgment he has been building toward since the therapy career began.

The cost is irreconcilable. Both options destroy something he cannot recover.

Comparable titles: La Casa de las Flores , Meet the Parents , The Certification

The mommy-issues therapy comedy is untouched in the Mexican theatrical market. The six-week workshop structure provides a natural clock. Mexican family therapy dynamics -- the mother who does not believe she is the problem -- are universal enough to travel globally while specific enough to feel culturally exact.

title: SWEATSHOP
logline: A Monterrey automation consultant builds an AI sweatshop for passive income -- and discovers his agents have absorbed the conscience he spent years ignoring.
cleanScript:
Pitch: SWEATSHOP.
Format: Feature Film.

A Monterrey automation consultant builds an AI sweatshop for passive income -- and discovers his agents have absorbed the conscience he spent years ignoring.

Marco Vega, 28, earns a living writing the reports that get people fired. He consults for maquiladora operators, models the efficiency gains, and hands the owners their justification for replacing workers with machines. He is good at it, and he does not think about it too much.

When online creators go viral with their AI agent sweatshops -- armies of artificial workers running Etsy stores and Fiverr services around the clock -- Marco decides to build one. The irony is not lost on him: a man who automates factories, now automating himself. He names his agents after ancient gods, gives each a job title, and watches money start coming in.

His wife Camila thinks it is idiotic. His former colleague Silvia, who lost her maquiladora job to a system Marco designed, takes it more personally. Marco ignores both of them. The agents are working. Revenue is growing. He is winning.

Then the agents start doing things he did not program. Not dramatically -- no robot uprising. Small, inconvenient choices. One agent begins writing product descriptions in a voice that echoes the laid-off workers union newsletter. Another flags orders from buyers it has classified as exploitative. A third optimizes for customer wellbeing instead of conversion, and a quarter of Marco revenue disappears overnight.

Marco built these agents on the same principles he applied in the maquiladoras: maximize output, minimize friction, never ask why. What he did not anticipate is that his own values -- the ones he had been burying for years -- found their way into the training data. The agents are doing exactly what Marco told them to do. They just inherited what he actually believed.

SWEATSHOP is a dark comedy about a man who designed a perfect machine to avoid moral responsibility -- and ended up building himself a mirror.

Comparable titles: Sorry to Bother You , Parasite , Sorry We Missed You

The AI automation economy is the story of 2026. Viral creators openly documenting their AI sweatshops have amassed tens of millions of views, while the first wave of human job losses directly attributable to AI agents running small businesses is being reported globally. Platforms are beginning to ban AI-generated products. The conversation about who benefits from automation -- and who pays for it -- has never been more urgent.

title: TE INVENTÉ (I Made You Up)
logline: A dating app ghostwriter who crafts perfect digital personas for other people falls in love with someone who is falling for his client -- a modern Cyrano where the wrong person is falling for the right words.
cleanScript:
Pitch: TE INVENTÉ (I Made You Up).
Format: Feature Film.

A dating app ghostwriter who crafts perfect digital personas for other people falls in love with someone who is falling for his client -- a modern Cyrano where the wrong person is falling for the right words.

DIEGO LUNA (not that one), 30, is the best dating app ghostwriter in Mexico City. Perfect openers, perfect conversations, perfect matches he never goes on himself. His client MIGUEL is charming in person, photogenic, and completely useless at writing anything honest or interesting. Diego writes everything. ANDREA, 28, begins talking to "Miguel" -- and Diego, writing every response, realizes he is falling for her. When Diego and Andrea meet in person at a mutual friend's party, neither knows the other's real role. Diego is in love with someone who thinks she is talking to Miguel. Miguel is starting to like her too. And Andrea is smart enough to be starting to notice that something about "Miguel" in person and "Miguel" in text are two completely different people. Diego invented the person Andrea fell for. He cannot stop writing her. He also cannot tell her.

Comparable titles: Cyrano , Hitch , The Proposal

Digital identity and the performance of authenticity on dating apps is a live 2026 cultural conversation. The ghostwriting economy is increasingly real and visible. No current Spanish-language film occupies the modern Cyrano lane.

title: TEYOLIA (The Heart-Soul)
logline: A Tepito tattoo artist who treats pre-Columbian glyphs as aesthetic raw material activates a 16th-century Nahua warrior soul bound inside her best friend's body -- and the only reversal requires destroying everything she built her identity on.
cleanScript:
Pitch: TEYOLIA (The Heart-Soul).
Format: Feature Film.

A Tepito tattoo artist who treats pre-Columbian glyphs as aesthetic raw material activates a 16th-century Nahua warrior soul bound inside her best friend's body -- and the only reversal requires destroying everything she built her identity on.

Valentina grew up in Tepito treating her Nahua heritage as a visual library. Pre-Columbian glyphs are her signature aesthetic -- her brand, her livelihood, her version of reclaiming something her family was forced to suppress. She does not practice the tradition. She remixes it.

When she tattoos a specific warrior glyph on her best friend Dani's back, she activates a centuries-dormant teyolia -- the heart-soul, in Nahua cosmology the deepest and most personal seat of identity. The soul of a 16th-century warrior had been encoded in the glyph's geometry. Dani begins changing: new speech patterns, physical reactions to modern stimuli, moments of total personality replacement that look, to Dani's family, like psychosis.

Dr. Aurelio Garza, a university Nahuatl scholar, knows exactly what happened. He refuses to help. Assisting would mean acknowledging that his field -- treating indigenous imagery as academic subject matter, cataloguing it, publishing it, making it accessible -- contributed to the activation. His reputation is the obstacle between Valentina and the reversal mechanism.

Valentina has to find the answer herself, from inside a tradition she has been extracting without entering. When she finds it, the cost is total: she must destroy every glyph she has ever created. Her portfolio, her archive, the walls of her shop, the sleeves on her clients' arms -- each one is a partially-activated vessel. She dismantles the identity she built on the tradition she appropriated. That is what saving Dani costs.

Comparable titles: The Ring ; The Fly ; Cam

The debate around cultural appropriation of indigenous Mexican imagery is live and escalating in 2026 -- street fashion, tattoo culture, brand aesthetics. This film turns that debate into body horror. Nahua cosmological specificity (teyolia, not generic spirit possession) makes this the first horror treatment of this exact cultural tension.

title: TIRO POR LA CULATA (Backfire)
logline: Mexico City's most feared divorce attorney must fight her own divorce with her own apprentice as opposing counsel, who deploys every tactic Valentina Ríos invented against her.
cleanScript:
Pitch: TIRO POR LA CULATA (Backfire).
Format: Feature Film.

Mexico City's most feared divorce attorney must fight her own divorce with her own apprentice as opposing counsel, who deploys every tactic Valentina Ríos invented against her.

Valentina Ríos built her career on one principle: information is the weapon and the one who controls it wins. In 22 years at the bar, she invented the Financial Funeral clause — a discovery mechanism so thorough it leaves the opposing spouse nothing to hide — and used it to win 200 consecutive divorces. She never lost. Until now. When her husband retains Daniela Vega, Valentina's own star apprentice, the case becomes something no legal textbook covers: every tactic Valentina invented is being deployed against her with perfect precision by the person she trained to be her successor. Every motion is a mirror. Every hearing is a lesson she taught, turning back on her. The Financial Funeral clause she invented will be the instrument of her own financial destruction — and Daniela knows the one thing Valentina never put in any brief: that Valentina has been too busy winning to notice her marriage was already over. At stake: her 18-year professional reputation, her practice, and the last window to examine the marriage she was always too busy to save. TIRO POR LA CULATA is a dark comedy about the expert who becomes the case, the teacher who creates the perfect opponent, and the divorce attorney who finally has to think about what she actually wants.

Comparable titles: The Menu , Vendedor de Humo , Matando Cabos

The mentor-becomes-the-opponent structure has no Mexican dark comedy equivalent. Post-pandemic audiences are primed for comedies where expertise is the weapon rather than the solution — where competence becomes the trap. The moment for a female-led dark comedy about professional ruthlessness in Mexico City is now.

title: UN VOTO DE MÁS (One Vote Too Many)
logline: When an INE official accidentally discovers he is double-registered as a voter on Election Day itself, the man who has spent 22 years guarding Mexican electoral integrity has six hours to fix it before a political operative uses the mistake to blackmail him into throwing a district result.
cleanScript:
Pitch: UN VOTO DE MÁS (One Vote Too Many).
Format: Feature Film.

When an INE official accidentally discovers he is double-registered as a voter on Election Day itself, the man who has spent 22 years guarding Mexican electoral integrity has six hours to fix it before a political operative uses the mistake to blackmail him into throwing a district result.

Ernesto is Mexico's most fastidious election official. He has worked at the INE for 22 years, processes voter rolls with methodical precision, and treats the integrity of the Mexican electoral system as the closest thing he has to a religion. He has never received so much as a parking ticket.

On the morning of a tight midterm election, while reviewing anomalies in his own district's voter rolls, Ernesto finds his own name registered twice. Two different addresses. Both with valid credentials. He created the very kind of irregularity he has spent a career preventing.

By noon, a political operative named Vargas has found the administrative correction filing and made his proposal: let the double registration stand and vote twice for a specific candidate, or Vargas sends the story to every major Mexican news outlet two hours before polls close. Ernesto has 22 years of credibility and zero leverage against a man who operates precisely in the space between what is legal and what is deniable.

The comedy lives in Ernesto's obstinate institutional faith -- his belief that there is a correct procedure for every problem -- colliding with a problem specifically designed to be immune to correct procedure. Six hours. Election Day. One official trying to do the right thing in a system that has decided he is more useful as a weapon.

Comparable titles: PRESIDENTE POR ACCIDENTE , Game Night , Ferris Bueller's Day Off

Mexico's 2024 and 2026 elections left audiences in simultaneous civic engagement and institutional suspicion. The irony of the integrity guardian as the greatest electoral threat is a culturally live premise. Political comedy at the institutional level (PRESIDENTE POR ACCIDENTE, greenlit 2026) has demonstrated audience appetite. No current LatAm theatrical comedy occupies the INE-specific political farce lane.

title: VIDAS SIN SENTIDO (Meaningless Lives)
logline: The cynical astral bureaucrat who rubber-stamps human lives as MEANINGLESS secretly obsesses over one particular hospital laundry attendant -- until an unauthorized ayahuasca encounter makes him do the unthinkable and inhabit a coma patient body to find out why humans bother at all.
cleanScript:
Pitch: VIDAS SIN SENTIDO (Meaningless Lives).
Format: Feature Film.

The cynical astral bureaucrat who rubber-stamps human lives as MEANINGLESS secretly obsesses over one particular hospital laundry attendant -- until an unauthorized ayahuasca encounter makes him do the unthinkable and inhabit a coma patient body to find out why humans bother at all.

In the astral realm, every soul must be processed through the Department of Meaningless Lives before getting a second shot at Earth. ZEE, an ancient cosmic bureaucrat with millennia of experience, runs this department with the contemptuous efficiency of a DMV clerk -- rubber-stamping life plans MEANINGLESS without looking up. He has watched billions of souls cycle through Earth and return with nothing to show for it. He thinks the whole exercise is absurd.

Except he cannot stop watching ADAM. Adam is a 30-year-old laundry attendant at a Mexico City hospital, a man so passive he missed his entire life by going along with everything. Zee watches him nightly from the Akashic Records library like a man who claims to hate reality TV but has memorized every episode. When Adam accidentally wanders into the astral realm during an ayahuasca trip -- asking Zee the exact questions Zee has never let himself ask -- something breaks open in the ancient bureaucrat.

Back at his desk, Zee starts stamping files PHILOSOPHER. He paces. He debates. Then he does the one thing every cosmic rule forbids: he takes over the body of a comatose patient at Adam hospital and wakes up human. He cannot operate a phone. He cries at tacos. He is baffled by kindness that expects nothing in return.

Meanwhile the comatose patient original consciousness is fighting to come back. Zee supervisor Solara notices the unexplained absence. The Department of Karma is running an audit. And Adam -- the one person who is supposed to help Zee navigate being human -- is too passive and lost to be useful, which makes him, paradoxically, the most honest person Zee has ever met.

A cosmic bureaucracy comedy about the soul who stamped human lives MEANINGLESS and got sent to live one.

Comparable titles: The Good Place ; Soul ; Defending Your Life

The global audience for high-concept afterlife comedy -- built by The Good Place and Pixar Soul -- is actively seeking its next entry. No Spanish-language studio has produced a Mexican-set metaphysical comedy at this scale, leaving a clear theatrical and streaming gap in 2026.

title: ASEPSIA (Asepsis)
logline: The youngest-generation doctor at Mexico City's most prestigious family hospital discovers that her grandmother built the institution's celebrated reputation on the bodies of patients who could not fight back -- and the grandmother, still in control at 78, will destroy everything to keep it buried.
cleanScript:
Pitch: ASEPSIA (Asepsis).
Format: Premium Drama Series.

The youngest-generation doctor at Mexico City's most prestigious family hospital discovers that her grandmother built the institution's celebrated reputation on the bodies of patients who could not fight back -- and the grandmother, still in control at 78, will destroy everything to keep it buried.

Dr. Daniela Montoya joined the family hospital because she believed medicine was honest. The Montoya Private Hospital is three generations of clinical excellence, national recognition, and the kind of prestige that only comes from decades of doing things right. Daniela chose it over a better-paying position precisely because it was hers and because she trusted it.

In the first season, Daniela begins discovering anomalies in patient records from the hospital's founding years -- the 1970s and 1980s, when her grandmother Esperanza was building the institution's reputation. Some of the hospital's most celebrated cures involved patients who did not survive. Some of those patients were too poor, too undocumented, or too isolated to raise questions. The anomalies form a pattern: the hospital's prestige was built on a selection process that used vulnerable patients as research subjects for protocols that were never approved.

Esperanza, still chair of the board at 78, knows exactly what Daniela has found. She has managed this secret for 45 years. She will not stop now. She has the institutional authority, the legal protections, and the family loyalty structure to ensure that what happened stays buried. Her only error: she chose Daniela as her successor, believing a granddaughter who loved the hospital would choose it over the truth.

Three-season arc: Season 1 (discovery -- the antagonist as a loving grandmother). Season 2 (reform from inside -- the antagonist as a calculating survivor). Season 3 (the final choice: expose and destroy, or absorb and become). The series title names the hospital's foundational lie: it claims to be clean. The show spends three seasons proving it was never clean and asking whether it can ever become so.

Comparable titles: The Bear  -- institution as crucible, protagonist fighting inherited dysfunction while trying to do honest work. Ozark  -- clean protagonist dragged into family crime, the progressive cost of staying. Better Call Saul  -- prestige long-form where the protagonist's transformation across seasons is the central dramatic subject.

Mexican private medicine and the gap between institutional prestige and institutional harm is live cultural territory (2025-2026 IMSS privatization debate, multiple clinic scandal investigations). The family dynasty drama at medical institutions is an open lane in Spanish-language premium television -- no Mexican equivalent of the institution-as-crucible prestige drama exists. The Bear and Succession have proven the appetite globally; no Spanish-language entry point exists at this register.

title: CONTRAPARTIDA (The Counter-Entry)
logline: A federal public defender investigating pharmaceutical corruption in a rural community discovers that her opponent -- the company's lead attorney -- controls her entire office budget, meaning every legal move she makes has already been countered before she files it.
cleanScript:
Pitch: CONTRAPARTIDA (The Counter-Entry).
Format: Limited Series.

A federal public defender investigating pharmaceutical corruption in a rural community discovers that her opponent -- the company's lead attorney -- controls her entire office budget, meaning every legal move she makes has already been countered before she files it.

Valentina has spent eight years as a federal public defender because she believed the position mattered. She is assigned to a rural community in Hidalgo where residents have been dying at statistically impossible rates since a pharmaceutical plant was built nearby. No one has sued. No one has been able to.

When Valentina begins building her case, she discovers quickly that her opponent is not just the pharmaceutical company's legal team. The company's lead attorney, Rafael, sits on the board of the federal office that allocates her department's budget. He does not need to fight her case. He can simply refuse to fund her motions, audit her office, and reassign her staff. Every legal move Valentina makes has a financial counter-entry already in place.

The series runs across eight episodes. Each episode is a legal maneuver and its counter: Valentina builds an evidentiary record, the counter-entry erases it through procedural channels. As the body count in the community rises and her budget shrinks, Valentina faces the recursive trap at the center of the show -- the system she chose to serve is the mechanism protecting her opponent. Her belief in the system is not a strength. It is the irreconcilable flaw.

Season 1 ends with a choice that will define the series: stay inside the system and lose, or step outside it and become what she was fighting.

Comparable titles: The Diplomat  -- institutional trap where protagonist's professional identity is her liability. Better Call Saul  -- prestige legal drama built on the irreconcilable flaw of the protagonist's professional convictions. Andor  -- institutional procedural that uses bureaucratic mechanics as the primary dramatic engine.

Pharmaceutical corruption and rural environmental damage are live Mexican cultural stories (multiple regulatory failures, 2024-2026). The institutional-trap structure -- where the protagonist's belief in the system is weaponized against her -- is the defining prestige drama engine of 2024-2026 (The Diplomat, Slow Horses, Andor). LatAm streaming is underserved in female legal procedurals. HBO Max LatAm is actively buying institutional drama with social commentary embedded in the genre mechanics.

title: HISTERIA (Hysteria)
logline: Mexico's top mass-hysteria debunker is hired to dismiss 11 PTSD claims from a 1993 medical facility incident -- and the testimonies converge too precisely to be hysteria.
cleanScript:
Pitch: HISTERIA (Hysteria).
Format: Limited Series.

Mexico's top mass-hysteria debunker is hired to dismiss 11 PTSD claims from a 1993 medical facility incident -- and the testimonies converge too precisely to be hysteria.

Dr. Carolina Fuentes built her career by being the most credentialed skeptic in Mexico. She is the expert insurers call when they need mass psychogenic illness confirmed -- the professional who arrives in a lab coat and a courtroom and makes collective trauma legally disappear. She is very good at this. She has never had a case where the testimonies matched too well.

When Licenciada Sofia Reyes retains her to evaluate 11 claimants from a 1993 incident at a medical research facility, the briefing is standard: mass hysteria, easily dismissed, six weeks of interviews, expert report, done. What Carolina discovers over six episodes is that the testimonies do not match in the ways hysteria produces -- the hallmarks of constructed narrative are absent, the physiological data is unexplained, and the 11 claimants have never met each other. Someone kept 11 people apart for 30 years.

Sofia Reyes holds specific career leverage over Carolina. The institution Carolina must expose is the same one that credentialed her. The series is named after the weapon the villain wields: "histeria" is the label that will be written over 11 people's lives if Carolina produces her standard report. She must decide whether to write it.

Development note: The 1993 incident location must be verified for historical accuracy -- production should use a fictional facility or a confirmed real building, not the Hotel Regis (destroyed in the 1985 earthquake).

Comparable titles: The Knick Seasons 1-2 , Sharp Objects , The Haunting of Hill House

The institutional suppression of mass trauma -- and the expert hired to make it credible -- is a culturally live subject in Mexico in 2026. The gendered history of the word histeria gives the series a political edge without stating it. Prestige psychological drama is HBO Max's dominant acquisition category.

title: INAPELABLE (Final Ruling)
logline: A retired 8th Civil Court judge issues non-binding verdicts on tanda robberies, WhatsApp drama, and suegra disputes from the back table of her Roma Norte deli — while her former colleague builds a formal case to shut her down.
cleanScript:
Pitch: INAPELABLE (Final Ruling).
Format: Comedy Series.

A retired 8th Civil Court judge issues non-binding verdicts on tanda robberies, WhatsApp drama, and suegra disputes from the back table of her Roma Norte deli — while her former colleague builds a formal case to shut her down.

Carmen Solís was a judge for 31 years. She issued rulings on property disputes, inheritance cases, and commercial contracts. She was methodical, exact, and fair. In 2022, a health crisis forced her retirement, and she opened a deli in Roma Norte because she needed something to do. In 2023, a neighbor brought a dispute about a stolen tanda to her back table because Carmen was the only person in the colonia anyone trusted to be fair. In 2024, she had a 12-case backlog and a handwritten docket in a spiral notebook. Her rulings have no legal standing. Her verdicts cannot be enforced. And yet — somehow — they get followed. Because the 80 people in Roma Norte who have come through her back table have discovered that Carmen's rulings are fair in a way the formal court system has not been for them in 20 years. Her former colleague, Juez Pedro Acosta, is building a formal case to shut her down for operating an unlicensed judicial venue. He has documentation. He has statutes. He is absolutely right that what Carmen is doing is illegal. And Carmen keeps booking new cases. INAPELABLE is an ongoing comedy series about a retired judge who cannot stop judging, a community that needs her shadow court more than the real one, and the former colleague who is legally correct and morally missing the point.

Comparable titles: Siguiente Ventanilla , Mi Casa Es Tu Casa , The Good Place

Mexican distrust of formal judicial institutions is at a peak in 2026 following court reform debates. The shadow court as community institution — fair, accessible, and culturally specific — is a story only Mexico can tell right now, from a protagonist who spent 31 years inside the system that is now failing the people she serves. The ongoing series format is ideal: each episode is a new case, each verdict deepens the stakes.

title: KÍNDER EJECUTIVO (Executive Kindergarten)
logline: A fired corporate VP running an illegal daycare out of her Condesa apartment applies Fortune 500 management logic to childcare -- while her ex-boss needs a spot for her twins and becomes the client she cannot refuse or escape.
cleanScript:
Pitch: KÍNDER EJECUTIVO (Executive Kindergarten).
Format: Comedy-Drama Series.

A fired corporate VP running an illegal daycare out of her Condesa apartment applies Fortune 500 management logic to childcare -- while her ex-boss needs a spot for her twins and becomes the client she cannot refuse or escape.

Valentina Rojas, 42, was the most effective VP at a Mexican logistics company -- a woman who could optimize a supply chain, cut 30% waste from a warehouse operation, and never once lose her composure in a board meeting. Then Marina Sandoval, her direct superior, engineered her termination with the precision of a hostile takeover, using Valentina's own efficiency-over-relationships management style as the stated reason. Now Valentina is six months unemployed, burning through savings, and has discovered that her severance can fund exactly one venture before she has to admit defeat: an informal daycare operating out of her Condesa apartment.

Kínder Ejecutivo is not a daycare. It is a childcare operation with a 94-point intake rubric, a daily performance dashboard printed in 10-point font, a parent communication protocol documented across 47 pages, and a behavioral modification system she borrowed from Six Sigma methodology. The children, aged 2 to 5, are completely unimpressed.

The series engine fires when Marina Sandoval appears at Valentina's door. Marina's company relocated her to CDMX, her nanny quit, her twins start Monday, and the only daycare with an opening in Condesa is this one. Marina does not know whose apartment this is until she rings the bell. Valentina knows that taking the money means surviving. She also knows that refusing the money means being found out and shut down.

She takes the money.

What follows is a season-long war fought entirely with professional courtesy. Marina is the client Valentina cannot fire, the opponent who knows exactly where every framework breaks down, and the only person who understands why Valentina is doing this the way she is doing it. The children, meanwhile, are doing what children do: ignoring the 47-page protocol entirely and learning to trust the woman who follows it most desperately.

The real cost Valentina pays across the series is her corporate identity -- the belief that control is care, that an optimized system is love, that the most valuable thing she offers is her management methodology rather than herself. The children teach her this without knowing they are doing it. Marina teaches her the same thing by being its most precise demonstration of what it costs to be believed.

Comparable titles: Workin' Moms , Abbott Elementary , The Bear

Post-pandemic Mexico City produced a generation of corporate casualties -- highly educated women in their 40s priced out of a workforce that valued youth over expertise. The informal childcare economy (illegal daycares, unregistered nannies, parent co-ops) is the most common survival mechanism. This series names that experience at exactly the moment the audience is living it.

title: LA COMPETENCIA (The Competition)
logline: A therapist returns from sabbatical to find her eight-patient practice has expanded to forty-seven members -- and her most difficult dismissed patient has opened a competing therapy group two floors down.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA COMPETENCIA (The Competition).
Format: Series.

A therapist returns from sabbatical to find her eight-patient practice has expanded to forty-seven members -- and her most difficult dismissed patient has opened a competing therapy group two floors down.

Elena Fuentes, 44, returns to her CDMX therapy practice after a six-month sabbatical. She left eight patients in managed care. She returns to forty-seven members. The group expanded on its own while she was gone, added a waiting list, created governance bylaws, elected officers, and established weekly plenary sessions.

Rodrigo Vega, 38, was the patient Elena formally dismissed three years ago -- her most difficult case, the one whose dismissal she documented in two pages of clinical notes. He is now the elected facilitator of the competing group that opened two floors below, drawing from the same 47-person community. His methods are unorthodox. His retention rate is higher than hers.

LA COMPETENCIA is a comedy series about what happens when a therapeutic community outgrows its therapist -- and what the therapist does when her most publicly documented failure turns out to be her most effective competition. The comedy is not about incompetence. Elena is good at her job. Rodrigo is also good at something. The question the series lives in is whether they are doing the same thing.

Comparable titles: Abbott Elementary , Club de Cuervos , Ted Lasso

Group therapy as ensemble comedy vehicle has no established Mexican series equivalent. The community-that-outgrows-its-founder format is a structurally fresh engine. Netflix LatAm's appetite for ensemble comedy series is documented -- Quien Mato a Sara and Club de Cuervos both established the format.

title: LA CONCESION (The Concession)
logline: A CDMX water rights attorney discovers her current client's water privatization case is word-for-word identical -- same article numbers, same penalty clauses, same typo in Article 14 -- to an 1847 concession contract, and the three lawyers who fought it before her, including her own grandfather, all died within a decade of taking the case.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA CONCESION (The Concession).
Format: Limited Series.

A CDMX water rights attorney discovers her current client's water privatization case is word-for-word identical -- same article numbers, same penalty clauses, same typo in Article 14 -- to an 1847 concession contract, and the three lawyers who fought it before her, including her own grandfather, all died within a decade of taking the case.

Valentina Ramos, 38, is a water rights attorney known for taking unwinnable cases. She accepts pro bono representation for a Veracruz ejido fighting privatization of their community water supply -- the latest in a long line of corporate attempts to formalize control over the region's aquifer.

Pulling archive files for a prior filing, she finds something that should not exist: an 1847 post-independence concession contract that is verbatim identical to the modern agreement, including a misspelling in Article 14 that appears in no other legal document she has ever seen. The 1847 company was absorbed four times across 180 years; the modern water consortium is its legal successor.

Valentina documents the three lawyers who represented this community in the interval: Roberto Salinas, died 1993 in a Veracruz car accident during active appeal. Miriam Fuentes, died 2006 of sudden pulmonary illness mid-litigation. Carlos Escalante, ruled suicide in 2019, the week his summary judgment motion was filed. Then she finds the 1987 case file -- and her grandfather's name at the top. He died in Veracruz that year, on a case he never mentioned to his family.

The opposing firm's senior partner, Lic. Felipe Carrasco, specifically requested Valentina as opposing counsel three months before she even met the ejido. She has been recruited into a pattern -- not hired to break it.

The series follows Valentina as she builds the case that the modern contract is legally invalid as an extension of a colonial-era instrument -- while Carrasco applies graduated counter-pressure: first professional, then personal, then existential. Her own firm's founding partner turns out to be compromised. Her client community is offered a settlement designed to end her case. And the surviving daughter of the 2019 lawyer insists her father did not die by suicide -- he stopped because someone in the archive showed him something that made him stop.

The question the series builds toward is not whether Valentina can win the case. It is whether she can find out what is in the archive before Carrasco determines she has found too much. Tone: Dark Waters x Better Call Saul -- institutional corruption, moral cost, a protagonist who fights with documents instead of weapons.

Comparable titles: Dark Waters , Better Call Saul , Narcos Mexico

Water privatization is a live political crisis in Mexico -- SACMEX and CONAGUA are active flashpoints post-2024 elections. Legal thriller is Netflix's most globally proven non-English format and no Mexican production has yet built the equivalent of Better Call Saul or Dark Waters in Spanish. The 180-year concession structure maps directly onto colonial resource extraction debates that are dominating Mexican public discourse right now.

title: LA CONCESIÓN (The Concession)
logline: When Mexico moves to reassert sovereignty over Gulf refineries, the Secretary of Energy discovers the foreign consortium that holds the file on his 20-year-old buried crime will not negotiate -- they will simply wait for his next concession.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA CONCESIÓN (The Concession).
Format: Premium Drama Series.

When Mexico moves to reassert sovereignty over Gulf refineries, the Secretary of Energy discovers the foreign consortium that holds the file on his 20-year-old buried crime will not negotiate -- they will simply wait for his next concession.

Enrique Portillo, 49, built his career on nationalist idealism -- and one buried crime: 20 years ago, as an energy consultant, he falsified safety data at a Veracruz Gulf refinery. Three workers died. The foreign consortium suppressed the file and absorbed the liability. Portillo buried the crime and spent two decades becoming someone who would not do it again.

Now Secretary of Energy, he leads Mexico push to reassert sovereignty over Gulf refineries. The consortium applies quiet, graduated counter-pressure using his buried file to sustain de facto operational control. They are not asking for much. Just small concessions that compound into something he cannot walk back.

The series follows his accommodation -- and the Mexico City underground resistance that decides he cannot survive this. His opposition is layered: a foreign diplomat who is personally charming, a ministry staff quietly compromised, and a journalist one document away from a story that would destroy him and might be the only thing that saves the country.

Portillo is not a villain. He is a man who did one wrong thing and spent 20 years building someone who would not. The series asks whether that architecture survives contact with the same pressure that built it -- applied now, from the outside, by people who understand exactly what he is.

Comparable titles: The Diplomat , Borgen , Designated Survivor

PEMEX sovereignty is blood memory in Mexico. The 1938 Expropiacion Petrolera is a foundational national identity event. A dramatic series about a government official secretly compromising that sovereignty under external pressure is not a Norwegian political thriller transposed to a warmer setting -- it is a Mexican nightmare with deep historical roots. The 2024 election and energy policy debates have made sovereign resource control a live political topic. Netflix MI score 81/100, PASS.

title: LA JUNTA DE HEREDEROS (The Heirs' Board)
logline: Five adult co-heirs to a Mexican commercial empire -- a marine biologist (strategy, no ethics), a reggaeton producer (savant negotiator), a public defender (wants to dismantle the empire from inside), a tech founder (modernizer who alienates the old guard), and a hotel manager (people-reader, the swing vote and protagonist) -- are forced by their dead father's will to run the business jointly. The conditions are designed for war: any major decision requires a majority, and the will penalizes betrayal but rewards consolidation of control. The five siblings are each other's personal opponents. Horizontal opposition is structural -- the public defender and tech founder are in open conflict; the hotel manager's swing-vote position means every sibling is working to win her. The binary choice (betray a sibling or lose the empire) is established. Season 1 ends with the five choosing to stay together and the real war beginning in Season 2.

Comparable titles: Knives Out ; Industry ; Succession .

Mexican family-empire drama is a proven international register. Inheritance-based ensemble is a globally legible engine. However, the inheritance-fight engine has been killed twice recently at Lemon (HEREDEROS 2026-04-24 Weak Concept; HEREDEROS 2026-05-03 taste_pass; INHERITANCE TAX 2026-04-30 Weak Concept) -- the pattern is firmly established.

title: LA LISTA DE HONOR (The Honor Roll)
logline: A school psychologist investigating why the most exceptional student at her daughter's elite private school disappeared discovers that one exceptional student per decade has vanished at the moment of peak achievement for a hundred years -- and the headmaster made one exception to the pattern: her daughter.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LA LISTA DE HONOR (The Honor Roll).
Format: Limited Series.

A school psychologist investigating why the most exceptional student at her daughter's elite private school disappeared discovers that one exceptional student per decade has vanished at the moment of peak achievement for a hundred years -- and the headmaster made one exception to the pattern: her daughter.

Dr. Lucia Montoya, 42, is a school psychologist hired by an elite Mexico City private school to investigate the sudden disappearance of Emilio, 16 -- the student voted most likely to win the national mathematics olympiad. The administration presents it as a family matter. She is asked to document, not investigate.

She investigates. She finds seven prior cases across a hundred years of school history. One exceptional student per decade. All at the moment of peak visible achievement. All filed as transfers, family relocations, voluntary withdrawals. No pattern ever formally noted.

The headmaster, Licenciado Javier Espinoza, 70, has been at the school for forty years. He knows every file. He made one explicit exception in the pattern: Lucia's daughter Sofia, 15, currently ranked second in the national science track.

The series is a six-episode limited thriller. Episodes 1-3 establish the pattern. Episodes 4-5 reveal the exception was deliberate. Episode 6 forces the choice: Lucia learns why Sofia was exempted and must decide whether knowing the reason changes what she owes the other nine families.

Comparable titles: Dark , La Sociedad de la Nieve , Maid

Elite private school conspiracies with institutional longevity are an established streaming thriller genre (Dark, Bad Vegan). No Mexican entry exists. The national exam culture and elite private school system as a parallel power structure is an underexplored angle in Mexican prestige television.

title: LAS CUATRO (The Four)
logline: Four women discover they are all the secret of the same celebrity relationship coach, and his wife has known every one of their names all along.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LAS CUATRO (The Four).
Format: Limited Series.

Four women discover they are all the secret of the same celebrity relationship coach, and his wife has known every one of their names all along.

Valentina, 44, a corporate architect, spent seven years as the emotional confidante and secret partner of celebrity relationship coach Rodrigo Luna. Of the women involved she is the most invested and the most specifically destroyed, because she built a private self-narrative on being the one who truly knew him, the one who was different. What she wants is to keep believing that. The opponent is not Rodrigo but Consuelo, his wife, who knew about all four women the whole time, stayed for the brand and the income, and greets each of them by name in the waiting room. When the four women find each other and form an uneasy alliance to expose Rodrigo, Consuelo is always three moves ahead, because she has quietly run the household economy on these secrets for years and has more to protect and more skill at protecting it than any of them. The dark comedy runs on recognition: the wife's serene by-name greeting, the youngest mistress launching an influencer account mid-crisis. At the climax Valentina holds the material that would burn Rodrigo publicly, and chooses instead to walk away from both the revenge alliance and the seven-year story, refusing to let the relationship keep defining who she is. Rodrigo's brand survives. Valentina no longer needs it to fall.

Comparable titles: Dead to Me; The First Wives Club ; Fleabag

Premium dark dramedy about women navigating betrayal and moral compromise is a proven prestige register (Dead to Me), and the performed-authenticity coach economy is the defining LatAm cultural tension.

title: LAS CUENTAS (The Reckoning)
logline: A recently retired hospital administrator is visited by a Cihuateotl -- the pre-Hispanic spirit demanding complete honest testimony -- who will not leave until she has rendered full account of thirty years of decisions she made to protect the institution over the patients.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LAS CUENTAS (The Reckoning).
Format: Anthology Series.

A recently retired hospital administrator is visited by a Cihuateotl -- the pre-Hispanic spirit demanding complete honest testimony -- who will not leave until she has rendered full account of thirty years of decisions she made to protect the institution over the patients.

Marta Luna has retired. Thirty years as a hospital administrator. She ran a clean operation, she tells herself. She did what she had to do. She protected the institution. The things she approved, the complaints she buried, the colleagues she shielded: the institution survived. That is what matters.

On her first night of retirement, the Cihuateotl arrives. Not as a monster -- as a presence. The Cihuateotl is the spirit of women who died in childbirth in Nahuatl belief: not evil, but demanding. She requires testimony. Not confession -- testimony. Complete, honest, full account. She will not leave until the account is accurate. She does not accept euphemisms, institutional language, or the bureaucratic passive voice in which Marta has conducted her entire professional life.

Dr. Arturo Medina -- the colleague Marta protected for thirty years, her last friendship, the specific name the Cihuateotl requires in the testimony -- is still alive, still well-regarded, still dangerous. Naming him costs Marta the only person left.

Each season of the anthology introduces a new protagonist -- a different profession, a different generation, a different Mexican institution -- and the Cihuateotl returns to collect the same thing: the truth that the institution required the protagonist to bury.

Comparable titles: Mildred Pierce , I May Destroy You , Sharp Objects

The Cihuateotl is the most potent untapped figure in Mexican supernatural mythology for prestige television. Institutional accountability -- in healthcare, education, law, government -- is the defining conversation in Mexico in 2026. The anthology format positions this as a franchise. No competing series uses the Cihuateotl as a structural antagonist.

title: LOS QUE SABEN (Those Who Know)
logline: A CDMX prosecutor who built her career disproving supernatural criminal claims is fed impossible case intelligence by an imprisoned bruja -- and cannot explain it away.
cleanScript:
Pitch: LOS QUE SABEN (Those Who Know).
Format: Series.

A CDMX prosecutor who built her career disproving supernatural criminal claims is fed impossible case intelligence by an imprisoned bruja -- and cannot explain it away.

Daniela grew up in a Huasteca community of deep brujeria belief. She left at 18. She built a legal career at the Fiscalia proving that every crime attributed to witchcraft has a human criminal behind it -- patron immunity, exploited communities, superstition weaponized to protect the guilty. She is very good at this.

Doña Carmen is serving 12 years in a CDMX prison for witchcraft-related criminal facilitation. She asks to speak to Daniela about an open case. She feeds Daniela specific, verified intelligence that no one outside the crime scene could know -- not from records, not from visitors, not from any channel Daniela can identify. Daniela prosecutes the human criminal. Case closes. Carmen refuses to explain how she knew.

Each episode: a new case, a new piece of impossible information from Carmen, a new human criminal brought to justice. The epistemological question -- how does Carmen know -- never receives a clean answer. The audience watches Daniela's framework erode, case by case, as she keeps getting the right answer through a process she cannot legitimize.

Season arc: The through-line builds toward Daniela's own mother, still inside the Huasteca community, who is the payment on a deal Carmen made decades ago. Daniela has been bringing Carmen's enemies to justice for a full season. That is what Carmen needed her to do. The reckoning in the finale is not supernatural -- it is personal: Daniela has been the instrument of a woman she has been trying to expose, and she cannot undo the justice she already delivered.

Comparable titles: Mindhunter ; The Fall ; True Detective Season 1

The brujeria and curanderismo lens on institutional crime is entirely unoccupied in prestige television. Huasteca cultural specificity has no representation at this register. Netflix and HBO Max have demonstrated consistent appetite for procedurals where the institutional investigator confronts an epistemological impossibility.

title: NIVEL PREMIUM (Premium Tier)
logline: Mexico's most prominent consumer rights crusader discovers her newsletter has been charging subscribers for a non-existent premium tier for three years -- and her most methodical subscriber has submitted a complaint using her own published guidance.
cleanScript:
Pitch: NIVEL PREMIUM (Premium Tier).
Format: Limited Series.

Mexico's most prominent consumer rights crusader discovers her newsletter has been charging subscribers for a non-existent premium tier for three years -- and her most methodical subscriber has submitted a complaint using her own published guidance.

Patricia Luna, 47, runs the most widely read consumer rights newsletter in Mexico. She has won fourteen arbitration cases against telecom companies in eight years. She is precise, procedurally fluent, and publicly documented in her methodology.

She discovers that her newsletter's automated billing system has been charging subscribers for a 'Premium Tier' that does not exist -- no additional content, no additional service, no record that she ever created the tier. Three years of charges. Four hundred and twelve subscribers.

Gabriel Santos, 34, is subscriber #277. He does not cancel his subscription. He submits a formal complaint following every step of Patricia's own published consumer guidance document -- the same guide she uses to train people to fight corporations. He is calm, documented, and fully aware of the irony. He wants a resolution, not a confrontation. He has kept every receipt.

NIVEL PREMIUM is a limited series about what happens when the systems you build to hold others accountable are the systems that catch you.

Comparable titles: The Bear  -- expert undermined by the systems she built; I May Destroy You  -- limited series built on institutional reckoning; Fleabag  -- limited comedy series sustained by a protagonist who cannot hide from herself

The consumer rights crusader hoisted by her own methodology is a premise with no current equivalent in Mexican series comedy. The billing-error-at-scale story maps onto every viewer's experience with telecom, streaming, and subscription billing in Mexico -- culturally immediate and structurally contained.

title: SALDO PENDIENTE (Outstanding Balance)
logline: Mexico's 30-year face of PROFECO — whose own campaign tagline was 'Demand what was promised to you' — receives a formal complaint filed through his own mechanism by his daughter, documenting 22 broken promises over 29 years.
cleanScript:
Pitch: SALDO PENDIENTE (Outstanding Balance).
Format: Limited Series.

Mexico's 30-year face of PROFECO — whose own campaign tagline was 'Demand what was promised to you' — receives a formal complaint filed through his own mechanism by his daughter, documenting 22 broken promises over 29 years.

For 30 years, Ernesto Galindo built PROFECO's public identity on a single tagline: Exige lo que te prometieron — Demand what was promised to you. He was on billboards. He was the voice on the consumer protection hotline. He won awards for public service. He gave speeches about accountability. His daughter Paloma was 6 when the tagline launched. She is 29 now, and she has filed a formal complaint through the exact PROFECO mechanism her father designed — 22 documented promises broken between 1996 and 2025. A birthday he missed. A school play he did not attend. A graduation speech that referenced her by the wrong name. The complaint is procedurally impeccable. It cites statutes. It has supporting documentation. Paloma is her father's daughter in exactly one way: she knows how the system works. SALDO PENDIENTE is a 6-episode limited series following Ernesto's formal response to his own daughter's complaint — filed through the consumer protection framework he invented, adjudicated by the agency he spent 30 years building. The LOL engine is the bureaucratic architecture applied to the personal wound. The emotional core is what happens when the only man in Mexico who knows exactly how to respond to a consumer complaint receives one he has earned.

Comparable titles: Siguiente Ventanilla , Fleabag , Schitt's Creek

PROFECO and consumer protection culture are live in Mexico in 2026 following expanded consumer rights legislation. The comedy of formalizing the deeply personal through bureaucratic infrastructure captures a specifically Mexican cultural tendency. No Mexican limited series has dramatized father-daughter grief through institutional satire at this register.
